title: #product: 6378901234
preview:
Priya Desai: Quick sync — we have a customer asking for an end-to-end pilot of FastRoute in ~2 weeks. Main blockers: infra prewarm (reserved capacity), runtime quantization/profile, and KMS/HSM key flow. I think we should decide staged scope today. TL;DR: staging-only pilot first, HSM later. Thoughts?
Alex Chen: Staging-only sounds safest. Runtime can cut a quantization profile and a conservative batching default, but 2 weeks is tight. Can we limit telemetry and non-blocking features? :thinking_face:
Samir Patel: Capacity note: to guarantee latency SLOs we need to reserve a small GPU pool and prewarm it for the pilot window. That requires a capacity request and cost sign-off. 2 weeks -> we need the request by EOD to make it.
Jordan Blake: Security: HSM/KMS review + customer key ownership is non-trivial. We can run without HSM in staging if the customer accepts envelope keys, but production (or anything with BYOK) needs the KMS review and attestation.
Riley Gomez: Customer preference is end-to-end with keys, but they said they'd accept staging-only to validate latency and UX. Product priority is verifying p95 latency and core correctness, not BYOK yet.
Alex Chen: Proposed rollout plan (small):
```
rollout:
  stage1: staging-only (no HSM), canary 10% internal, reduced telemetry
  stage2: HSM-enabled (BYOK) after KMS review, full telemetry
runtime_configs:
  default_batch: 32
  model_variant: quantized-v2
  fallback: fp16-v1
```
Samir Patel: Ok — if we do staging-only, infra needs a pinned pool for the pilot window and a jittered prewarm schedule. I'll file the capacity request. @Priya please confirm expected pilot traffic shape (qps, concurrency).
Priya Desai: Expected pilot traffic: ~150 qps peak, concurrency spike to ~60. We'll provide a small synthetic trace. Priorities: p95 < 250ms, correct fallbacks.
Jordan Blake: I'll kick off the KMS/HSM intake with security docs but it will slip past the 2-week pilot unless we deprioritize other reviews.
Alex Chen: For rollout safety, add this fallback rule: `if gpu_latency > 500ms -> route to fp16-v1`. Also enable KV prefix caching for common prompts. That helps p95.
deploy-bot: scheduled prewarm job created: `prewarm-fastroute-637890` -> starts in 36h (automated).
Riley Gomez: Decision call: staging-only pilot in 2w with reserved capacity + reduced telemetry. HSM/BYOK moved to next cycle (Q3). Action items: @Samir file capacity request by EOD, @Jordan start KMS intake, @Alex ship quant profile + fallback, @Priya send traffic trace. Agree? :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: Filing capacity request now.
Jordan Blake: Starting KMS intake, will post checklist.
Alex Chen: I'll drop quant profile PR today and add the fallback rule.
Priya Desai: Sending traffic trace in #product-docs in 15m.
Riley Gomez: Great — locking staging-first. We can reconvene mid-week if anything blocks. Thanks everyone. :rocket:

title: #product: 6001234567
preview:
Alex Kim: Q2 flightcheck — short status for OKR milestone owners. Reply with one-liners: shipped / slipped / next-step. Please include any blocking deps or PRs that need reviews. Aiming to lock the OKR doc by EOD Friday. :airplane:

Maria Gonzales: runtime-v3 autoscale — shipped ✅ Canary at 50% for web tier. No major regressions so far. PR: https://github.com/redwood/runtime/pull/421 needs review (mainly infra test changes). Next: roll to 100% after Chen reviews.

Chen Li: I'll review PR421 this afternoon. Heads-up: one e2e test flakes on Dedicated env — that's the slipped bit. Blocking dep: eng-infra needs the new GPU image (ticket #PLAT-87). Next-step: fix flake + coordinate image bump with infra.

Samir Patel: console telemetry retention feature — slipped. Storage budget exceeded during load test. Short-term mitigation: retention toggle + backfill compaction job. PR: https://github.com/redwood/console/pull/88 (needs Jordan). Owner: Samir. ETA: patch and canary next Tue.

Jordan Blake: Picking up the console PR review now. Also raising infra quota symlink ticket — pinged eng-infra. If infra gives quota, can land in patch window.

deploy-bot: Scheduled canary deploy for runtime-v3 autoscale at 2026-03-22T18:00Z. Monitoring channels: #eng-oncall, #eng-runtime.

Maria Gonzales: Quick note — model-routing canary completed on EU region: no perf regressions. Shipped ✅

Chen Li: Dependency map: runtime-v3 autoscale -> infra image PLAT-87 -> quota (infra) -> network policy (security). I'll add this to the OKR doc.

Samir Patel: For PR reviewers: if you can pick a PR today, add :eyes: reaction. My PR88 needs one more approver from product or observability.

Jordan Blake: Adding my :thumbsup: — will approve once infra confirms quota.

Chen Li: quick log check:
```
kubectl -n runtime logs -l app=runtime --since=1h | tail -n 200
```

Alex Kim: Great. I'll aggregate these into the OKR doc and mark shipped/slipped. Pattern for doc: one line per owner: [component] shipped/slipped — short next-step + PRs + deps. Posting summary by 17:30 PST.

title: #postmortems: 4520000000
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick follow-up from the postmortem on the weekend autoscale spikes. We need owners, JIRA links, clear RACI and short ETAs. Keep items scoped small. Please add by EOD.
Mina Patel: I'll take autoscaler policy tuning (scale-in/scale-out thresholds + cool-down). Will open JIRA and add a small test plan. ETA: +3 working days. @Victor can you review the policy rollouts?
Victor Huang: I'll own the third-party latency fallback and circuit-breaker tuning. Also will add a synthetic probe to detect slow externaldependency. Creating tickets now.
SRE-bot: Suggested follow-up template attached -> https://infra.redwood/templates/postmortem-action
Mina Patel: Created RED-60100 (autoscaler policy + tests). Link: https://jira.redwood/RED-60100
Mina Patel: Acceptance criteria for RED-60100:
```
- New scale-out threshold reduces surge CPU-triggered bursts without increasing cost >2%
- Cool-down prevents flip-flop on repeated spikes
- Staging run (3/3) during simulated spike window
- Rollback procedure documented
```
Victor Huang: Created RED-60101 (third-party fallback) and RED-60102 (synthetic probe). Links: https://jira.redwood/RED-60101
Victor Huang: RED-60101 R=Victor, A=Victor, C=Mina, I=Platform
Victor Huang: RED-60102 R=Victor, A=Platform Observability, C=Victor, I=Oncall
Jordan Blake: Quick RACI proposal (please confirm):
- RED-60100: R=Mina, A=Mina, C=Victor, I=Eng SRE
- RED-60101: R=Victor, A=Victor, C=Mina, I=Third-party Vendor
- RED-60102: R=Observability, A=Observability, C=Victor, I=Oncall
Mina Patel: Confirmed. I'll add @Jordan as reviewer on RED-60100 and add a short rollback snippet.
Mina Patel: Rollback command (to be included in ticket):
```
- kubectl scale deployment web-server --replicas=3 --namespace prod
- revert autoscaler config: s3://infra-configs/autoscaler/prod/previous.json
```
Victor Huang: Confirmed. ETA proposals:
- RED-60101 (fallback + CB) due +2 days
- RED-60102 (synthetic probe) due +4 days
Jordan Blake: Monitoring/alert notes: add rolling median and 5m p95 panels, and an alert that fires if synthetic probe latency >300ms for 3 consecutive probes. Also add a tag "pm-weekend-spike" to all tickets.
Victor Huang: Added probe alert details to RED-60102 and tagged tickets.
ops-bot: Postmortem archived -> https://postmortems.redwood/4520000000; follow-ups attached
Jordan Blake: Summary of commitments:
- RED-60100 (autoscaler tuning) 1 owner Mina 1 due +3d
- RED-60101 (third-party fallback) 1 owner Victor 1 due +2d
- RED-60102 (synthetic probe + alert) 1 owner Observability 1 due +4d
Jordan Blake: I'll run a short checkpoint tomorrow morning. If anyone needs to reassign, update the JIRA and ping this thread. Thanks :thumbsup:

title: #docs: 3987654322
preview:
Ravi Nair: FYI I opened a docs PR to reconcile the parser heuristics in the function-calling guide and to patch a couple redirects. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4875 :mag_right:
Hannah Vu: Nice. Which pages moved? I clicked the link and got a 302 loop for the schema anchor.
Ravi Nair: The schema anchor changed when we split schema-defs.md into two pages. The PR updates links in docs/guides/function-calling.md and docs/snippets/js-tooling.md. Also standardizes examples to prefer explicit `action`+`args`.
Jordan Blake: I reviewed the examples. One thing: our parser fallback section only shows a naive regex (`/\{.*\}/s`) which can greedily capture multiple JSONs in long output. We should show a safer shim — e.g., look for the first balanced braces region or use a short-circuit that tries a streaming JSON parser.
Hannah Vu: Agreed. Can we add a tiny Python snippet that demonstrates a try-except fallback: parse JSON -> if fails, run `find_balanced` helper -> try parse again -> log and return best-effort. Keep it minimal though; docs should avoid shipping large utility code.
Ravi Nair: Good plan. Proposal: inline minimal helper and then link to a more complete gist. Something like:

```python
# minimal fallback: find first balanced {} region
def find_first_json(s):
    depth = 0
    start = None
    for i,ch in enumerate(s):
        if ch == '{':
            if depth == 0: start = i
            depth += 1
        elif ch == '}':
            depth -= 1
            if depth == 0 and start is not None:
                return s[start:i+1]
    return None

try:
    out = json.loads(resp_text)
except ValueError:
    candidate = find_first_json(resp_text)
    out = json.loads(candidate) if candidate else None
```

That demonstrates the heuristic without endorsing it as perfect.
Jordan Blake: Works for me. Also we should add a short JS equivalent and call out edgecases (embedded arrays of objects, multiple toolcalls in one reply).
Sekou Diallo: On the link fixes: I noticed a couple of internal anchors using absolute internal URLs which break on the static site deploy. I patched them in a separate commit in the PR. Also updated the redirect rule in the netlify config.
Hannah Vu: Small wording nit: the guide currently says "the model must emit JSON" which we've been trying to avoid. Could we change to "Prefer structured JSON output; if not possible, use the parser fallbacks below"? That softer tone helped legal/UX reviewers before.
Ravi Nair: Yes, updated wording in the PR description. Also added an "Advanced: compact envelope" subsection showing {"type":"toolcall","payload":{"action":"...","args":{}}} and a note that this is useful for routing but less human-readable.
docs-bot: snippet-ci ran on PR #4875 61; python-snippets: 1 failed, js-snippets: 0 failed. Logs: https://ci.redwood-inference.internal/snippet/4875
Jordan Blake: The failing python snippet was the one missing a language tag on the code fence. Quick fix: add ```python. I left an inline review comment.
Ravi Nair: Thanks Jordan, I pushed that. Also moved the full JSON schema into a collapsible block and linked to the canonical schema page so we don't duplicate the whole definition inline.
Sekou Diallo: Pinged infra to re-run the snippet job once the commit lands. Netlify redirect change also staged.
Hannah Vu: One more thought: add a tiny checklist at the bottom of the guide for docs authors: 1) use explicit action/args examples; 2) include regex/fallback only in "If non-JSON" section; 3) link to canonical schema, don't copy it; 4) run snippet-ci locally. This should reduce future regressions.
Ravi Nair: Love the checklist. I'll add it and then merge once snippet-ci is green. If anyone wants a quick review before merge, @Jordan @Hannah please take a final look.

title: #marketing: 3260000000
preview:
Riley Park: Idea: 20-min mini-lab + 20-min Q&A. Bundle = 1 short tutorial, a tiny refapp, and a live bench demo. Goal: dev signups + repo stars.
Jordan Blake: 👍 timeline?
Riley Park: Target May 24 webinar. Tutorial publish May 17, repo open May 20, promo starts May 10.
Maya Ortiz: I can write the tutorial (hands-on, <700 words). Will include quick run steps: clone, set API key, run demo.
Chris Wang: I'll seed repo `community-mini-stream` with bench.py. Example to include in tutorial:
```
python bench.py --model community-stream --seq 512 --requests 30 --warmup 5
```
Outputs p50/p95 and tokens/sec.
Jordan Blake: Landing CTA = RSVP (email, role, company_size). Post-event CTA: repo + free hosted trial link. Add hidden `interest` (Hosted/Dedicated/Private).
Ops Bot: webhook test -> /integrations/hubspot/mini-lab -> ok.
Maya Ortiz: Messaging angle: "replayable benchmarks" + "build a streaming chat in 10 lines". I'll draft 3 headlines for A/B.
Riley Park: Backup plan: pre-recorded bench clip and fallback model `community-stream-small`.
Chris Wang: Ops note: reserve a small Dedicated slot for demo window. I'll coordinate.
Jordan Blake: Asset ask: hero image + 7s GIF. Design deliver by May 15.
Maya Ortiz: I'll drop draft May 12 for review. Chris/Jordan can review by May 14.
Riley Park: Perfect. Adding tasks to marketing board under "mini-lab: streaming + bench". Thumbs if good. :thumbsup:
Riley Park: :thumbsup:

Maya Ortiz: :thumbsup:
Chris Wang: :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 3166000000
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick thread 1: Partner wants a structured private-offer waterfall (commit bands + step-down renewals) and expects to be able to on-board SI activation early. Ops/legal/finance need rules.
Mateo Ruiz: TL;DR: we can support tiered discounts but need fixed metadata for marketplace. also partner requests automatic migration credit if customer converts within 60 days.
Sam (Legal): Must include explicit renewal waterfall in the private-offer terms. if it's not codified, renewals become ad-hoc. sample language: "Year1: X% off; Year2: Y% off; Year3: Z% off (automatic step-down unless renegotiated)". Also need confidentiality clause until listing.
Aisha (Finance): Credits = contra-revenue per accounting. we require a hard cap per customer and global ceiling for exposure. propose cap = min($125k, 12% of committed ARR). will model sensitivity.
Priya Singh: Partner insists on early SI activation to demo to key accounts. ops question: do we allow usage to count toward the committed bucket pre-list?
Mateo Ruiz: We can allow it but only if partner provides signed SOW and we issue a staging SKU mapping token. record all usage and reconcile post-list.
Sam (Legal): Add appendix: SKU mapping + 60-day deprecation notice. include audit rights for usage reconciliation.
Jordan Blake: Marketing embargo: no partner-led public mentions until the marketplace listing is live. partners may share private previews under NDA.
market-bot: Created checklist: marketplace-waterfall/3166000000. Reminder: attach private-offer T&Cs to listing metadata.
Aisha (Finance): Finance needs clarity on how marketplace fee affects net economics (marketplace take). include an example calculation in the private-offer docs so partners understand net price to customer vs ARR impact.
Mateo Ruiz: Ops to-do: implement temporary mapping token flow:
```
1) partner submits mapping CSV
2) eng-ops issues temp SKU token for activation
3) usage logs tagged with token
4) on listing, swap token -> marketplace SKU and emit audit event
5) run reconciliation and apply credits if conversion criteria met
```
Sam (Legal): For migration credits: cap them, treat as contra-revenue, and include an expiry (60d). also require partner to confirm automation for credit application (webhook or file feed).
Priya Singh: SI enablement ask: 30-day window to complete runbook after the soft-list. partner wants access to docs earlier; we can provide a redacted runbook before sign-off.
Jordan Blake: Decision proposal:

- Count pre-list usage toward committed spend but require monthly reconciliation and audit rights
- Allow one-time migration credit on conversion (60d expiry) with finance cap

title: #partnerships: 2839990011
preview:
Maya Chen: FYI AcmeCloud PO #AC-2024-987 is blocked in the marketplace portal — portal rejects the invoice due to 'legal name/address mismatch'. Can procurement + legal take a look?
Raj Patel: Ugh. Is this the merchant alias vs legal entity issue again?
Maya Chen: Looks like marketplace expects 'AcmeCloud, Inc.' but the invoice uses 'AcmeCloud LLC' (they recently re-org'd). portal error: 'ENTITY_ID_NORMALIZATION_FAILED'.
Priya Singh: @Maya can you paste the raw payload that hit the portal? marketplace ops might need the exact field set.
Maya Chen: Here — payload snippet from PO system:
```json
{"po_id":"AC-2024-987","vendor_id":"ACME-0001","billing_entity":"AcmeCloud LLC","address":"1234 Cloud Way, Suite 200, Denver, CO 80202","tax_id":"12-3456789"}
```
Elena Gomez: We can't book the invoice until legal entity matches our vendor ledger. Finance can temporarily hold under 'AP-HOLD: vendor_alias' if legal signs a new W-9, but that's manual.
Tom Rivers: Need executed MSA amendment or updated W-9/EIN docs from the vendor. Without that we can't change ledger mapping.
AcmeCloud Sales: Sorry — we've been onboarding a new legal entity. I'll upload an updated W-9 and signed MSA amendment by EOD and ask marketplace to accept a merchant alias. Apologies for the churn.
Jordan Blake: If marketplace won't accept alias, we can add a temporary mapping in our ledger service to map 'AcmeCloud LLC' -> 'AcmeCloud, Inc.' but that requires a ticket and an audit trail. Example API call we used before:
```bash
curl -X POST https://internal-ledger.redwood/api/mappings \ 
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $LEDGER_KEY' \ 
  -d '{"external_name":"AcmeCloud LLC","canonical_name":"AcmeCloud, Inc.","reason":"marketplace alias temporary mapping"}'
```
Priya Singh: Marketplace support sometimes pushes back on manual mappings. I'll open a support case (ref: MSP-92431) and ask for an alias accept.
deploy-bot: PO ingestion failed for AC-2024-987 — reason: ENTITY_ID_NORMALIZATION_FAILED (ingest-id: igst-283999-12)
Raj Patel: If marketplace support is slow, can finance accept under the legacy vendor code + create a reconciliation ticket? What's the customer impact (billing/delivery)?
Elena Gomez: Customer-facing: none yet. We haven't invoiced. Risk: delayed payment to vendor and blocked deployment of reserved marketplace credits.
Tom Rivers: Legal: we can provisionally approve a mapping if vendor provides a scanned W-9 and the signed amendment. Need both to clear compliance.
AcmeCloud Sales: Uploading docs now: https://acmecloud.share/docs/w9_acmecloud.pdf and MSA_amendment_signed.pdf — please confirm receipts.
Maya Chen: Received links. @Priya please escalate MSP-92431 with attachments and ask for manual accept. @Jordan please standby for ledger mapping only after Legal confirms docs.
Priya Singh: Escalation created, pinged rep, and included uploaded docs. Marketplace replied: 'Need proof of EIN match + original PO signer info.' They want a notarized EIN affidavit for alias change (ugh).
Tom Rivers: Notarized affidavit is heavy — ask vendor if they can provide company bank statement + EIN confirmation letter from IRS (sufficient in many cases).
AcmeCloud Sales: Can provide bank confirmation + EIN letter. Will push notarized affidavit if required.
Jordan Blake: Small ops note — if we add a temporary mapping we should tag it with 'ttl:7d' and create a reconciler ticket to revert mapping after close. I can populate the ticket template.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Acme uploads bank + EIN letter (AcmeCloud). 2) Legal (Tom) to review docs ASAP. 3) Priya to press marketplace for alias accept and manual PO clear. 4) Jordan to prepare ledger mapping template but not apply until legal OK. 5) Finance (Elena) to prepare AP hold fallback.
Raj Patel: Thanks — let me know if I should loop in sales leadership for further escalation.
AcmeCloud Sales: We'll prioritize docs. ETA 3pm PT.
Tom Rivers: I'll review by 6pm PT and post checklist here.
Maya Chen: Great — marking this as blocking for the deal desk. Will follow up in 2 hours. :hourglass_flowing_sand:
Priya Singh: Marketplace ticket updated with attachments and escalated to partner support engineer. (MSP-92431 -> level2).
Elena Gomez: Finance set AP-HOLD (internal ref: APH-7782) as contingency.
Maya Chen: Thanks everyone. Next update when legal confirms or marketplace accepts.

title: #eng-platform: 2711112222
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick follow-up from the postmortem — two regressions escaped our canary yesterday. Need owners + short timelines for guardrails.
Priya Desai: I'll own rollout gating and blast-radius enforcement. Plan: require `max_blast_radius` on flag creation, add pre-merge lint. ETA: schema PR by Thu.
Markus Vogel: I'll take auto-rollback. Prototype a ruleset that evaluates error_rate+p95 and calls an idempotent `/rollouts/{id}/action` with `pause|throttle|rollback`. PoC by Fri.
Sara Kim: I'll handle telemetry and recurrence detection. I'll add: daily canary anomaly job, backfill 30 days for baselines, and two saved queries (`canary_spike_v2`, `p95_shift`). Dashboard + alerts to #eng-oncall.
Jordan Blake: Good. Please paste PR links here. Also scheduling weekly 30m follow-ups for 6 weeks.
Priya Desai: Proposed rollout snippet:

```yaml
rollout_policy:
  stages: [0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 1.0]
  max_blast_radius: 0.02
  monitor:
    error_rate: { threshold_pct: 1, window_s: 120, action: rollback }
    p95_latency: { threshold_ms: 500, window_s: 300, action: pause }
```
Markus Vogel: Implementation note: prefer soft-throttle on borderline triggers (0.1%) to gather more signal, then escalate to pause. All actions emit audit events.
Sara Kim: Will backfill and post dashboard links in #eng-observability. Alerts routed to owner + oncall.
rollout-bot: Reminder: "Rollout safety follow-ups" weekly Mon 10:00 for 6 weeks created.
Jordan Blake: Ownership matrix: Priya -> flags/schema (PR Thu), Markus -> rollback (PoC Fri), Sara -> telemetry (jobs Mon). Rotate primary monthly and appoint backups.
Priya Desai: Opening draft PR for flag schema: https://git.redwood/ri/flags/pulls/302 (WIP).
Markus Vogel: Draft PR for rollback engine: https://git.redwood/ri/guardrails/pulls/423 (WIP).
Sara Kim: Will ping when dashboards + alerts are live.
Jordan Blake: Thanks. React with :white_check_mark: once PRs are merged and I'll close the postmortem follow-up.

title: #eng-runtime: 2365550000
preview:
Jordan Blake: Starting thread to finalize PRD scope for kernel pluggability + KV policy split. Want minimal launch gates and clear ownership.
Priya Rao: Can we commit to splitting into two releases? KV defaults first, kernel selector second. Product needs conservative defaults for tenants.
Marta Gomez: Agreed. KV rollout items: per-tenant namespace ON by default, memory budget per-tenant, and tenant opt-out API. Add lifecycle key rotation on tenant delete.
Evan Li: For kernel selector, we need a compatibility matrix test and a graceful fallback path. Suggest runtime flag `--kernel-mode=stable|exp` and per-route annotation for opt-in.
Sasha Levin: Acceptance draft: 
- KV: default TTL 30m, LRU eviction, per-tenant memory guardrails, no cross-tenant reads.
- Kernel selector v0: support 2 kernel families, P95 latency <= +10% vs baseline for buckets [8,64,512], generation QA delta < 1.0% on core eval.
Jordan Blake: Ops rollout: canary progression 0.5% -> 3% -> 15% with 24h soak at 15% for KV; kernel selector gets 0.5% -> 5% -> 25% with 48h soak at 25% for high-risk models. Automated rollback if error >2x baseline for 15m.
Priya Rao: Observability must include kernel_selection.trace_id, kernel_version metric, and audit events for config changes. Product gating: audit logs + rollback <2m required for enterprise sign-off.
Marta Gomez: Edge cases to capture in RFC: mixed-batch bucketization by kernel capability, long-context eviction backstop for >2048 tokens, and custom CUDA op detection leading to auto-fallback.
Evan Li: CI/Perf: baseline harness = prod snapshot from 2026-02-08 run scripts. Perf jobs must publish raw histograms and per-bucket diffs. Add integration test that runs KV+selector together in smaller infra.
Sasha Levin: Security: namespace encryption at rest, key rotation policy, and tenant namespace deletion workflow.
InfraBot: deploy:canary created id=cp-2365550000, clusters=[us-east-1-canary], target=0.5%
Jordan Blake: Action items: Jordan update PRD + GH PR; Priya draft KV rollout playbook; Marta add compat matrix and tests; Evan add CI perf jobs; Sasha write observability + security checklist. Review on GH by Fri EOD.

title: #postmortems: 2290011223
preview:
Alex Nguyen: Following the model-fallback cascade postmortem, I'm collecting owners for the remediation items. Please claim and add Jira links. Target: owners + due dates by EOD Mon.

Alex Nguyen: Remediation list: 1) Add model-variant health checks + circuit-breaker thresholds. 2) Implement per-tenant fallback quotas to avoid cost blowouts. 3) Update rollout checklist to include load-profile validation. 4) Create retrospective queries to find past cascades and tag them. 5) Draft handoff playbook for oncall -> runtime when fallback triggers.

Marta Lopez: I can take #1 (health checks + circuit-breaker). Will spec thresholds and propose a lint rule to prevent missing checks in future deploys.

Marta Lopez: Jira: RUNTIME-6140 "variant healthchecks + cb". ETA: two weeks. Attaching a quick threshold sketch in the ticket.

Jordan Blake: I'll pick #2 (per-tenant fallback quotas) but need product on policy shape. @Alex Nguyen can you add the PM to the ticket?

Jordan Blake: Created ticket: COST-2233 "tenant fallback quotas (soft limits)". Leaving unassigned until policy approved.

Ravi Singh: I'll handle #3 (rollout checklist). I'll add a gating item: synthetic load run with target percentile latency. Also will add a step to validate memory/KV shard balance.

Ravi Singh: Ticket: SRE-4032 "rollout checklist: load and shard validation". ETA: 1 week.

Alex Nguyen: For #4 retrospective queries, I can help but recommend we add an automated tag when a fallback chain exceeds X downstream calls. OpsBot can we add an action?

OpsBot: Action scheduled: add auto-tag rule for fallback chains >3 hops (scheduled job 2290020000). Reminder set for owner check-in in 4 days (2290015600).

Marta Lopez: For the handoff playbook (#5) I'll collaborate with Ravi. Need to decide RACI for oncall vs runtime. Suggest: Responsible = oncall, Accountable = runtime lead, Consulted = model infra, Informed = product.

Jordan Blake: +1 to that RACI. Also propose a short escalation matrix in the playbook with contact rotation.

Alex Nguyen: I've looped PM (@Marta Lopez did she already add PM?) — PM assigned in COST-2233 as "product-cost". Jordan, once product confirms quota shape, please set due date.

Ravi Singh: Small note: we should backfill telemetry for June-July to measure frequency of cascades. Who can run those queries?

Jordan Blake: I can run queries if storage access is available. Will file a subtask under COST-2233.

Marta Lopez: One more: add canary check that simulates partial model unavailability (stub fallback) during deploys. This lives under RUNTIME-6140 as a test job.

Alex Nguyen: Good. Quick summary: owners assigned (RUNTIME-6140 Marta, COST-2233 Jordan, SRE-4032 Ravi), OpsBot automations scheduled, RACI agreed. I'll add these to the remediation tracker and ping oncall rotation for the playbook handoff.

Jordan Blake: ACK.

Ravi Singh: ACK.

Marta Lopez: ACK.

Alex Nguyen: Thanks everyone. If policy blocks slow you down, call it out in the ticket so PM / Eng M can triage.

title: #eng-oncall: 2240012345
preview:
Incident Bot: Paging: elevated token TPS and error 429s observed for model family 'nebula-13b' in us-west-2. Spike started at ~22:10 UTC, sustained. Alerts: per-model TPS > 4x expected, queue depth increasing :rotating_light:
Alex Chen: ack. I'm oncall. digging in.
Mira Patel: i'm loading recent metric dashboards — looks like a small subset of customers are hitting long-chat workloads, generating very large prefixes. TPM and RPM both high for nebula-13b.
Alex Chen: confirm 429s are coming from rate limits or from serving backpressure?
Mira Patel: quick check: origin is quota guard hitting per-model RPM caps and then triggering 429. Upstream serving latency also climbing.
Alex Chen: ok. We need a live mitigation plan: reduce per-model RPM for nebula-13b, enable fallback routing to nebula-8b, and temporarily disable streaming to reduce KV memory pressure. Thoughts?
SRE Lead: agree with that order. Start with RPM/TMP change (least customer-visible), then streaming toggle. We should document customer impact before changes.
Alex Chen: copying mitigation commands — using rdwctl (internal ops).
Alex Chen: ```bash
# emergency throttle to reduce per-model RPM/TPM for nebula-13b
rdwctl quotas set --model nebula-13b --tpm 120 --rpm 600 --note "emergency-surge-2240012345"
```
Ops Bot: ran: rdwctl quotas set returned job_id=job-99321 queued. Expected propagation ~15s.
Mira Patel: running quick canary: simulate a large prefix using loadtool against nebula-13b in staging. Waiting for quota to apply.
Alex Chen: also preparing fallback route: route policy to prefer nebula-8b for non-critical tiers.```yaml
# route update snippet
- match: model.family == "nebula-13b" and customer.tier != "enterprise"
  action: route-to: nebula-8b
  rollback: true
```
Jordan Blake: are we notifying any customers? Which ones are impacted?

title: #incidents: 2163456789
preview:
SRE Bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: dedicated-acme-eu pool GPU_mem_pressure >= 95% (5m) and queued_requests > 200; paging oncall.
Alex Chen: noticed a slow ramp starting ~09:02 UTC, sustained for 8m. Embedding refresh + nightly indexer for ACME kicking off. GPUs reporting high mem and kernel retries.
Maria Gf3mez: grafana snapshot: 6/6 nodes > 93% GPU_mem, avg latency spiking 3x, errors show `CUDA OOM` on a few worker pods.
Timothy Park: I'm oncall. Quick question: can we free any tenant-local state (KV caches, prefix cache) without infra restart?
Alex Chen: yes. We have a runtime command to evict tenant caches. Running `infractl cache evict --tenant=acme --scope=prefix:* --confirm` now (will affect warm starts).
Infra Admin Bot: cache-evict job accepted (job id: evict-9861). ETA ~45s.
Jordan Blake: Heads up: ACME's indexer is in their control plane; account team on a call says they can pause it for 30m if needed. They prefer we try in-place first.
Alex Chen: cache evict completed. Observed ~4-8% reduction in mem across nodes. Not enough alone.
Maria Gf3mez: autoscaler log shows failed scale due to ENI quota for VPC subnet (attach limit):
```
[2163456798] autoscaler: scale_request: +3 nodes
[2163456798] k8s-cloud: attach_network_interface failed: eni_limit_exceeded
```
Maria Gf3mez: also a protection flag blocked cross-region spill: `pool-policy: disallow-cross-region-burst=true` set by ops.
Timothy Park: options checklist: a) pause ACME indexer (fast) b) enable cross-region spill to dedicated-acme-us (adds latency) c) emergency burst toggle after infra approval (adds capacity in-region) d) force model fallback to lower-memory variants. Which first?
Jordan Blake: ACME ok with short pause to indexer if it solves it immediately. They want to avoid cross-region routing if possible.
Elaine Rivera: Account approves indexer pause and will sign emergency billing for burst if needed. Legal cleared.
Alex Chen: executing pause: `acmectl job pause --tenant acme --job indexer --reason "emergency capacity"`
Alex Chen: indexer job paused. Monitoring...
Maria Gf3mez: good drop in queue length. GPU_mem down ~6% more. Latency improving but still above normal.
Timothy Park: still seeing kernel retries for chat endpoints. Let's enable model fallback for embedding endpoints to quantized variant: `rictl model set-variant --tenant acme --endpoint /v1/embeddings --variant embed-q4_0 --rollout 100%`
Alex Chen: applied. Also lowering per-tenant streaming concurrency cap to 170: `rictl router set-policy --tenant acme --max-concurrency 170` to protect other tenants.
Infra Admin Bot: alert: infra-admins required to toggle `disallow-cross-region-burst`. Would you like me to page infra-admins?
Timothy Park: Page infra-admins. We may still need cross-region if mem keeps rebounding.
Infra Admin Bot: paging infra-admins group now.
Alex Chen: meanwhile I'm going to attempt a hot-swap of a few large KV caches to disk to free GPU host memory: `infractl kv-offload --tenant acme --threshold 500MB` (starting).
Maria Gf3mez: metrics: after cache offload + indexer pause + model fallback, GPU_mem at ~72% across nodes. Error rate dropped to baseline.
Jordan Blake: ACME confirms their dashboard shows errors cleared. They still want us to request quota increases to avoid repetition.
Elaine Rivera: I've got approval for a 3-hour emergency cross-region shift if infra requests it; billable. But prefer not to if current fixes hold.
Infra Admin Bot: infra-admin Alice accepted the page: investigating ENI quota and pool policy.
Alex Chen: proposing follow-ups: 1) quota increase request to cloud ops for ENIs & vCPUs 2) add per-tenant background-job soft-limits 3) add an automatic model-variant fallback when GPU_mem > 80% for >3m 4) one-click emergency burst toggle for pool owners.
Timothy Park: taking over monitoring. I'll keep burst options on standby. Handing to me for 45m watch.
SRE Bot: Incident acknowledged by oncall (Timothy Park). Severity: Sev2 (major customer degraded). Current status: mitigated (indexer paused + model fallback + cache offload). ETA to close: monitor 45m, then post-incident review.
Alex Chen: creating incident ticket INC-216345 and attaching graphs + runbook steps executed.
Jordan Blake: I'll prepare a short customer note summarizing actions and optional next steps (quota request + scheduled maintenance window).
Maria Gf3mez: adding action: implement per-tenant enqueue throttle for embeddings.
Timothy Park: will update thread if anything flares. For now, mitigations effective. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-platform: 1999991234
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick sync: product wants unversioned keys to prefer v2 by default. We need safety rails (fallback, quotas, console preview) before flipping.
Aisha Rahman: Agree but add per-route pins. Some customers rely on v1 wire shapes for /v1/embeddings and /v1/rerank.
Victor Chen: Ops concern: silent reroute is risky. Proposal: auto-fallback only when tenant enables `compat.auto_fallback`, otherwise return a 503 and surface a clear error code.
Nora Patel: UX: migration preview card should show per-route estimates: projected monthly token delta, p95 latency delta, and a small list of sample diffs. Offer a "Run ad-hoc shadow" button.
telemetry-bot: Metric proposal: redwood.migration.shadow.requests, .v1_count, .v2_count, .fallback_count, .token_delta_p50_p95, .semantic_regression_rate. Tag with shadow_run_id and migration_cohort.
Jordan Blake: Add header precedence doc: 1) explicit x-api-version or x-client-version -> honor, 2) token metadata (pinned_v1/pinned_v2), 3) tenant compat setting, 4) global default.
Aisha Rahman: For billing we need a shadow_estimate persisted in quota-service so console preview shows monetary impact and customers aren't surprised.
Victor Chen: Circuit breaker rules: if tenant_fallback_rate > 0.5% sustained for 10m OR global_fallback_rate > 0.25% for 2m -> disable auto-switch for impacted cohorts and page oncall. Add telemetry event redwood.migration.circuit_trip.
Nora Patel: Enterprise opt-out: console toggle `compat.exempt` with audit logs and bulk actions. Show a gentle banner: "We're preparing to route unversioned requests to newer models; preview impact before switching."
telemetry-bot: We'll backfill historical v1 traffic as cohort=0 for comparisons. Shadow runs should default to sample_pct=0.1 per-route.
Jordan Blake: Implementation notes: route-manager add enum compat_mode: pinned_v1, pinned_v2, prefer_v2, shadow. Add shadow_run API payload: { tenant_id, routes[], sample_pct, target, duration_hours } (UI will prefill safe defaults).
Aisha Rahman: SDKs: add compatibility note to ignore unknown fields; include migration notes in changelog.
sre-bot: Runbook: add steps for handling circuit_trip including rollback, throttle formulas, and investigation checklist. Recommend initial throttle cap = min(15%, fallback_rate * 8).
Victor Chen: Testing: e2e for header precedence, quota accounting with shadow_estimate, fallout telemetry, and load tests for circuit thresholds.
Nora Patel: Action items: Jordan - draft RFC + route-manager PR (due Wed). Aisha - telemetry metric names + dashboard mock (due Tue). Victor - circuit breaker thresholds + runbook (due Mon). Nora - console preview mock + enterprise opt-out flow (due Tue).
Jordan Blake: Thanks all. Let's run a 24h staging shadow next week at 1% tenants and reconvene with numbers.
Aisha Rahman: Sounds good :thumbsup:
Victor Chen: Will prepare load test scenario.
Nora Patel: I'll write banner copy and audit requirements.

title: #incidents: 1905007890
preview:
oncall-bot: INCIDENT alert 1905007890 — service degradation: text-gen endpoints (acmepay-prod) reporting high latency and intermittent 504s. paging @Samir (oncall).
oncall-bot: runbook link: https://redwood-internal/runbooks/kv-cache-eviction  — incident channel created.

Samir Patel: I'm on it. seeing 95th latency ~18s, error rate spike to 2.3%. initial hypothesis: KV cache thrash on the dedicated pool, GC spikes + model fallback causing long tail. scaling up pool now.

Liz Chen: pinged support and product ops, we have 3 customer tickets from AcmePay. they're seeing slow invoice generation. CC @Maria.

Maria Gomez: thanks. prioritizing outreach to affected accounts. Samir, any ETA for mitigation?

Samir Patel: scaled pool + forced warm replicas; started rolling restart for nodes showing high cache misses. early telemetry: errors down to 0.8%, p95 moving toward 7000ms. still higher than baseline.

Jordan Blake: I'm tracking logs, will run the request-replay against a canary cluster to reproduce. leaving notes for handoff at 07:00 UTC if needed.

Samir Patel: quick log excerpt: ```
[03:10:12] WARN kvcache: eviction count=1432, miss_rate=0.78
[03:11:03] ERROR serve/runtime: model-fallback triggered for route=/v1/generate, reason=kvcache-miss
[03:12:01] WARN infra: pod node-gpu-4 oom_kill_count=1
```

Liz Chen: good call to capture OOM/kills. does this map to any recent deploy? Release bot — any deploys to acmepay pool in the last 24h?

devops-bot: No production deploys to acmepay-prod in last 24h. Autoscaler bumped pool sizes at 02:58 UTC due to traffic surge.

Samir Patel: looks like traffic spike + previously noisy long-running requests caused the cache budget to be exhausted and evictions to cascade. fallback to smaller model increased per-request CPU, further exacerbating OOMs.

Maria Gomez: customer impact summary for postmortem: ~10% of AcmePay requests affected for ~22 minutes, some hit 504s. Support reported 3 high-priority cases, no chargebacks reported yet.

Liz Chen: scheduling postmortem: propose tomorrow 16:00 PT / 23:00 UTC. agenda: timeline, contributing factors, action items, customer communications. who can attend?

Samir Patel: I'll prepare the initial RCA timeline and upload a draft.

Jordan Blake: I'll own repro and the cache tuning experiment.

Alex Rivera: I'll own automated alert thresholds and will create a quick dashboard to monitor kvcache eviction rate by pool.

Maria Gomez: I'll handle customer comms and sign-off on impact language. please make sure support ticket links are in the doc.

Samir Patel: uploaded RCA draft: https://redwood-internal/rca/1905007890-draft — request reviews from @Liz, @Jordan, @Alex.

Liz Chen: reading now. quick comment: timeline misses the autoscaler bump at 02:58 — pls add. Also suggest an explicit rollback/fallback policy so we don't trigger heavier models automatically when cache pressure is high.

Samir Patel: noted and added. also added mitigation snippet to run as emergency runbook: ```
# emergency mitigation
kubectl scale deployment acme-gen --replicas=12 -n acmepay-prod
kubectl rollout restart deployment acme-gen -n acmepay-prod --field-selector=status.phase=Running
```

Jordan Blake: left inline comments about load pattern — attaching reproducer script: https://redwood-internal/tools/reproducers/acmepay-spike.sh. needs review for safety on prod.

Liz Chen: peer review checklist: 1) clear timeline, 2) impact numbers, 3) contributing factors, 4) action owners and due dates, 5) customer comms & postmortem note. once checklist done, we'll sign off.

Alex Rivera: created Jira RED-3421 to track remediation actions (link: https://jira.redwood/RED-3421). I've added sub-tasks: alert tuning, cache-reservation experiment, dashboard, postmortem meeting notes.

Samir Patel: proposed action items (with owners):

text:
oncall-bot: INCIDENT alert 1905007890 — service degradation: text-gen endpoints (acmepay-prod) reporting high latency and intermittent 504s. paging @Samir (oncall).
oncall-bot: runbook link: https://redwood-internal/runbooks/kv-cache-eviction  — incident channel created.

Samir Patel: I'm on it. seeing 95th latency ~18s, error rate spike to 2.3%. initial hypothesis: KV cache thrash on the dedicated pool, GC spikes + model fallback causing long tail. scaling up pool now.

Liz Chen: pinged support and product ops, we have 3 customer tickets from AcmePay. they're seeing slow invoice generation. CC @Maria.

Maria Gomez: thanks. prioritizing outreach to affected accounts. Samir, any ETA for mitigation?

Samir Patel: scaled pool + forced warm replicas; started rolling restart for nodes showing high cache misses. early telemetry: errors down to 0.8%, p95 moving toward 7000ms. still higher than baseline.

Jordan Blake: I'm tracking logs, will run the request-replay against a canary cluster to reproduce. leaving notes for handoff at 07:00 UTC if needed.

Samir Patel: quick log excerpt: ```
[03:10:12] WARN kvcache: eviction count=1432, miss_rate=0.78
[03:11:03] ERROR serve/runtime: model-fallback triggered for route=/v1/generate, reason=kvcache-miss
[03:12:01] WARN infra: pod node-gpu-4 oom_kill_count=1
```

Liz Chen: good call to capture OOM/kills. does this map to any recent deploy? Release bot — any deploys to acmepay pool in the last 24h?

devops-bot: No production deploys to acmepay-prod in last 24h. Autoscaler bumped pool sizes at 02:58 UTC due to traffic surge.

Samir Patel: looks like traffic spike + previously noisy long-running requests caused the cache budget to be exhausted and evictions to cascade. fallback to smaller model increased per-request CPU, further exacerbating OOMs.

Maria Gomez: customer impact summary for postmortem: ~10% of AcmePay requests affected for ~22 minutes, some hit 504s. Support reported 3 high-priority cases, no chargebacks reported yet.

Liz Chen: scheduling postmortem: propose tomorrow 16:00 PT / 23:00 UTC. agenda: timeline, contributing factors, action items, customer communications. who can attend?

Samir Patel: I'll prepare the initial RCA timeline and upload a draft.

Jordan Blake: I'll own repro and the cache tuning experiment.

# emergency mitigation
kubectl scale deployment acme-gen --replicas=12 -n acmepay-prod
kubectl rollout restart deployment acme-gen -n acmepay-prod --field-selector=status.phase=Running
```

title: #docs: 1829998888
preview:
Kai Moreno: Heads-up: product wants a short deprecation playbook we can link from the banner. Keep it tiny 	600 words max	: what to change, quick regexes, and a smoke checklist. Who can draft?

Leah Wong: I can draft the playbook. Plan: three sections - Quick fixes, Snippet swaps, Canary checklist. I'll include 2 regexes for bulk replace and a sample smoke script.

InfraBot: Note: please enumerate exact /v1 path prefixes we need to redirect. Provide them in PR comment as a JSON list. Infra will create redirect map once PR merges.

Jordan Blake: I'll update Swift + Kotlin SDK READMEs. Suggest showing a compact before/after snippet and a one-liner about streaming callback rename.

Maya Iqbal: Small ask: include an "eval smoke" bullet in the checklist. We should run a few generation prompts in the canary to sanity-check semantics (not just errors). Add 5 representative prompts.

Leah Wong: Good call @Maya. I'll add an eval section: 5 prompts + expected rough outputs and a pass/fail guidance (semantic drift > 20% triggers rollback).

Kai Moreno: For the bulk-replace regexes, prefer conservative patterns. Example suggestions: 
- `s/\bmodel_name\b/model/g`
- `s/\bonData\b/onDelta/g`
Put them in the doc and explain risk (false matches).

Jordan Blake: I'll mirror those in SDK PRs. Also planning a tiny unit test that asserts the new `responses.create` signature exists.

InfraBot: deploy-checks/1829998888: created smoke job template `playbook-migrate-182999`. Assign to PR merger.

Maya Iqbal: Also add a note about embeddings dim changes: if teams use offline indexers, include an "index re-embed" flag and a pointer to the embedding-dim migration guide.

Leah Wong: Will include. Action items I'll list at top of playbook: owner (Leah), reviewers (Jordan, Maya), smoke-template (playbook-migrate-182999). I'll open the PR and ping infra with the path list.

Kai Moreno: Nice. When PR lands, let's label it `migration:playbook` and add the tiny checklist comment: owner, reviewer, smoke-template. We'll merge after two approvals.

Jordan Blake: On SDKs I'll keep both v1 and v2 examples for 48h post-redirect, then remove v1 snippets. I'll add a short note in PRs about why.

Leah Wong: Draft ready by EOD. If anyone sees a tricky edgecase (webhook backcompat, embed dim) paste here and I'll fold it in. We'll reconvene tomorrow 9:30am PT to review the PR. :calendar:

title: #devex: 1820001111
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick thread  onboarding + SDK parity issue. New engineer did `redwood login` (writes ~/.redwood/token) then ran node sample and hit 401. Node SDK only checked REDWOOD_API_KEY. We should standardize precedence and improve diagnostics.

Priya Nair: Seen this pattern a few times. The inconsistency between SDKs is the root cause. We need: clear precedence, a diagnostic whoami, and a deterministic rotation flow.

Marcus Lee: My proposal (minimal, backward-compatible): precedence = 1) explicit client creds passed to constructor, 2) REDWOOD_API_KEY (env), 3) REDWOOD_SERVICE_TOKEN (env; service/project scope), 4) REDWOOD_TOKEN_FILE (env path or default ~/.redwood/token), 5) OS keychain/secret stores. Keep REDWOOD_ORG / REDWOOD_PROJECT only for routing.

Tasha Rivera: +1. Also add an opt-in client.metadata.auth_source string so samples and CI can assert what was used. Example: `client.metadata.auth_source === 'token_file'`. We must never log secrets, only the source enum.

Jordan Blake: For rotation: gateway should set `x-rw-auth-status: expired|revoked|invalid` on 401. SDKs: on expired -> try next source once, emit `auth:expired` event and surface a single actionable error with hint to `redwood login` or set REDWOOD_API_KEY; on revoked/invalid -> fail fast with `credentials-revoked` or `credentials-invalid`.

Priya Nair: Small UX win: sample apps print at startup -> "auth_source=token_file (from ~/.redwood/token)" or "auth_source=env (REDWOOD_API_KEY)". Also add `redwood whoami --json` that prints {org, project, auth_source}.

Marcus Lee: CI nuance: if CI env detected (CI=true or GITHUB_ACTIONS), default to prefer env and disable file fallback unless explicitly configured. Add option `preferEnvInCI` in CredentialProvider. Prevents accidental reliance on mounted token files.

Tasha Rivera: Implementation sketch for node SDK: a CredentialProvider chain with methods get(), sources(), on('fallback', cb). Example: `new CredentialProvider({order:['env','file','keychain'], preferEnvInCI:true})`. Expose `client.on('auth:expired', handler)` and `client.metadata.auth_source`. Unit tests for precedence + integration test mocking `x-rw-auth-status: expired`.

Jordan Blake: Security: telemetry on fallback should be opt-in and only include auth_source enum. No tokens, no org/project ids. Also mask anything that looks like a token in logs.

Priya Nair: Docs copy for quickstart: "If you used `redwood login`, the CLI token file will be picked up by SDKs (unless CI prefers env). To force env use `export REDWOOD_API_KEY=...`. Run `redwood whoami` to see the effective credentials." Add migration note in console after change.

Marcus Lee: I'll take node PR: CredentialProvider + refreshOn401 + startup logging for samples. Will include tests and a small whoami shim in the samples repo. Priya, can you draft the spec for python parity?

Priya Nair: Yep. Will draft and share link here.

auth-bot: Created ticket RDW-11022: "SDK credential precedence + whoami + auth-status header + CI env behavior" assigned to @Marcus Lee.

Tasha Rivera: Acceptance checklist: spec doc, node provider + tests, sample whoami + startup log, CLI whoami implementation, gateway header for auth-status. If we land all, onboarding tickets should drop.

Jordan Blake: Great. Please ping when PRs are open so support can validate in staging. Thanks!

title: #support: 1813456789
preview:
Jordan Blake: escalating on behalf of Acme Payments. They report a spike in 5xx/403 for EU users since ~03:15Z and claim requests are being routed to us-east where token validation fails. sample request id=01H8KQ8R

Kai Chen: can you paste the exact curl the customer used and any response headers or trace id?

Jordan Blake: copied from customer:

```
curl -v -H "Host: api.acme.com" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
  https://api.redwood.example/v1/payments/charge
```

response headers (customer):
```
HTTP/2 403
x-region: us-east
x-envoy-upstream-service-time: 312
server: envoy
```

Jordan Blake: they insist traffic should land in eu-west. token issuer is eu-kms and when routed to us-east auth fails.

Anya Patel: did we touch any host/route rewrite logic recently? I remember a change for cookie sticky sessions last week.

Kai Chen: scanning deploys... edge-proxy-4.12 rolled 2026-02-11T03:18Z. PR #2548 introduced host_rewrite for legacy backends. pulling config dump now.

Ingrid Moss: created INC-2026-443, tagging infra + eng-platform. Org: acme-42. SLA P1.

sre-bot: INC-2026-443 created by Ingrid Moss  incident channel: #incidents  runbook: gateway-region-mismatch

Kai Chen: envoy config snippet from front-proxy pod:

```
virtual_hosts:
  - name: "acme-vhost"
    domains: ["api.acme.com"]
    routes:
      - match: { prefix: "/" }
        route:
          cluster: "cluster_us_east_payment"
          host_rewrite: "us-east.internal.redwood"
          request_headers_to_add:
            - header:
                key: "x-routing-timestamp"
                value: "%START_TIME%"
```

Kai Chen: looks like host_rewrite is applied at vhost level for api.acme.com  that would force origin to the us-east cluster regardless of x-client-region.

Jordan Blake: our region routing relies on x-client-region set by the edge LB. host_rewrite would bypass that and send EU tokens to US validators.

Anya Patel: why was host_rewrite added in PR #2548? It was supposed to be scoped to legacy orgs only, to work around backends that expect a fixed Host header. maybe the matcher is too permissive.

Kai Chen: repro locally: send X-Client-Region: eu-west to edge-proxy -> upstream cluster chosen: cluster_us_east_payment and upstream decorator shows host_rewrite=us-east.internal.redwood

```
# repro
POST /v1/payments/charge HTTP/1.1
Host: api.acme.com
X-Client-Region: eu-west
Authorization: Bearer <token>

> upstream: cluster_us_east_payment (host_rewrite -> us-east.internal)
< HTTP/2 403
< x-region: us-east
```

Kai Chen: mitigation options: revert host_rewrite or tighten route matcher to require an org-level legacy flag header (e.g., x-org-legacy: true).

Anya Patel: short-term patch: add a header condition to the route so host_rewrite only applies when x-org-legacy=true. canary the change to 2 pods now.

Ingrid Moss: pinged Acme with ETA: "Rolling targeted patch, estimate 15-30m to canary and validate."

Jordan Blake: note: Dedicated customers may see cross-region latency when failover hits us-east. we've observed p95 jumps due to cross-region hops. keep an eye on retries that could affect billing/duplicate charges.

Kai Chen: canary deployed to edge-proxy-4.12-canary-1/2. initial traces show requests with X-Client-Region: eu-west now route to cluster_eu_west_payment and token validation succeeds. sample p95 improved from ~1.2s -> ~210ms on these traces.

Anya Patel: opening PR to revert broad host_rewrite and add stricter matcher + unit tests. will push full rollback in 5m if canary looks good.

Jordan Blake: Acme reports success: "200s coming through, thanks" :thumbsup:

sre-bot: incident INC-2026-443 status -> mitigated

Ingrid Moss: follow-ups: 1) revert host_rewrite commit + add unit tests for region-preserving routing 2) add integration test simulating X-Client-Region + legacy flag 3) postmortem scheduled tomorrow 10:00 UTC

Kai Chen: leaving trace/log for postmortem:
```
[2026-02-11T03:25:11Z] request_id=01H8KQ8R host=api.acme.com x-client-region=eu-west -> upstream=cluster_us_east_payment host_rewrite=us-east.internal.redwood
```

Anya Patel: PR opened: https://git.redwood/edge/PR/2602 (revert host_rewrite, add matcher). linking postmortem after closure.

Jordan Blake: tagging @support-leads to update Acme escalation notes. no billing impact flagged yet; monitor for retry-induced duplicate charges.

Ingrid Moss: will close loop with Acme in 30m and run postmortem tomorrow 10:00 UTC.

Kai Chen: recommend infra guardrail: lint rule that prevents host_rewrite from being applied when it would change region for orgs with region pins. I'll prototype a rule for the config linter.
text:
Jordan Blake: escalating on behalf of Acme Payments. They report a spike in 5xx/403 for EU users since ~03:15Z and claim requests are being routed to us-east where token validation fails. sample request id=01H8KQ8R

Kai Chen: can you paste the exact curl the customer used and any response headers or trace id?

Jordan Blake: copied from customer:

```
curl -v -H "Host: api.acme.com" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
  https://api.redwood.example/v1/payments/charge
```

response headers (customer):
```
HTTP/2 403
x-region: us-east
x-envoy-upstream-service-time: 312
server: envoy
```

Jordan Blake: they insist traffic should land in eu-west. token issuer is eu-kms and when routed to us-east auth fails.

Anya Patel: did we touch any host/route rewrite logic recently? I remember a change for cookie sticky sessions last week.

Kai Chen: scanning deploys... edge-proxy-4.12 rolled 2026-02-11T03:18Z. PR #2548 introduced host_rewrite for legacy backends. pulling config dump now.

Ingrid Moss: created INC-2026-443, tagging infra + eng-platform. Org: acme-42. SLA P1.

sre-bot: INC-2026-443 created by Ingrid Moss  incident channel: #incidents  runbook: gateway-region-mismatch

Kai Chen: envoy config snippet from front-proxy pod:

```
virtual_hosts:
  - name: "acme-vhost"
    domains: ["api.acme.com"]
    routes:
      - match: { prefix: "/" }
        route:
          cluster: "cluster_us_east_payment"
          host_rewrite: "us-east.internal.redwood"
          request_headers_to_add:
            - header:
                key: "x-routing-timestamp"
                value: "%START_TIME%"
```

Kai Chen: looks like host_rewrite is applied at vhost level for api.acme.com  that would force origin to the us-east cluster regardless of x-client-region.

Jordan Blake: our region routing relies on x-client-region set by the edge LB. host_rewrite would bypass that and send EU tokens to US validators.

Anya Patel: why was host_rewrite added in PR #2548? It was supposed to be scoped to legacy orgs only, to work around backends that expect a fixed Host header. maybe the matcher is too permissive.

Kai Chen: repro locally: send X-Client-Region: eu-west to edge-proxy -> upstream cluster chosen: cluster_us_east_payment and upstream decorator shows host_rewrite=us-east.internal.redwood

```
# repro
POST /v1/payments/charge HTTP/1.1
Host: api.acme.com
X-Client-Region: eu-west
Authorization: Bearer <token>

> upstream: cluster_us_east_payment (host_rewrite -> us-east.internal)
< HTTP/2 403
< x-region: us-east
```

Kai Chen: mitigation options: revert host_rewrite or tighten route matcher to require an org-level legacy flag header (e.g., x-org-legacy: true).

Anya Patel: short-term patch: add a header condition to the route so host_rewrite only applies when x-org-legacy=true. canary the change to 2 pods now.

Ingrid Moss: pinged Acme with ETA: "Rolling targeted patch, estimate 15-30m to canary and validate."

Jordan Blake: note: Dedicated customers may see cross-region latency when failover hits us-east. we've observed p95 jumps due to cross-region hops. keep an eye on retries that could affect billing/duplicate charges.

Kai Chen: canary deployed to edge-proxy-4.12-canary-1/2. initial traces show requests with X-Client-Region: eu-west now route to cluster_eu_west_payment and token validation succeeds. sample p95 improved from ~1.2s -> ~210ms on these traces.

Anya Patel: opening PR to revert broad host_rewrite and add stricter matcher + unit tests. will push full rollback in 5m if canary looks good.

Jordan Blake: Acme reports success: "200s coming through, thanks" :thumbsup:

sre-bot: incident INC-2026-443 status -> mitigated

Ingrid Moss: follow-ups: 1) revert host_rewrite commit + add unit tests for region-preserving routing 2) add integration test simulating X-Client-Region + legacy flag 3) postmortem scheduled tomorrow 10:00 UTC

Kai Chen: leaving trace/log for postmortem:
```
[2026-02-11T03:25:11Z] request_id=01H8KQ8R host=api.acme.com x-client-region=eu-west -> upstream=cluster_us_east_payment host_rewrite=us-east.internal.redwood
```

Anya Patel: PR opened: https://git.redwood/edge/PR/2602 (revert host_rewrite, add matcher). linking postmortem after closure.

Jordan Blake: tagging @support-leads to update Acme escalation notes. no billing impact flagged yet; monitor for retry-induced duplicate charges.

Ingrid Moss: will close loop with Acme in 30m and run postmortem tomorrow 10:00 UTC.

Kai Chen: recommend infra guardrail: lint rule that prevents host_rewrite from being applied when it would change region for orgs with region pins. I'll prototype a rule for the config linter.

title: #finance: 1795123456
preview:
Jordan Blake: Heads up — Customer CloudBright flagged a surprise charge on invoice INV-2026-0589. They say a 15% promo was applied even though the promo window ended Jan 31 and this usage is Feb.
Nina Soto: ugh  can you attach the invoice lines and promo code?
Jordan Blake: INV-2026-0589 lines:
- On-demand tokens: 1,200,000 @ $0.0008 = $960.00
- Promo: PB-FEB15 applied (-15%) = -$144.00
- Net = $816.00
Promo meta: PB-FEB15 valid 2026-01-10 -> 2026-01-31. Customer usage 2026-02-05.
Billing Bot: Auto-validate: promo expired but promo applied by reseller integration. Created ticket FIN-3299. Recommend: issue credit memo for $144 and notify partner to stop backdated promo windows.
Evan Cho: Do we need a goodwill extra for the customer? They mentioned impact on their month-end close.
Nina Soto: I think credit memo is enough. No cash refund. Keep note: this was partner-side misconfiguration.
Jordan Blake: OK will create CM-2026-0811 for $144 and cc partner ops. Proposed customer note: "We removed an incorrectly applied expired promotion from invoice INV-2026-0589 and issued a $144 credit memo. We apologize for the confusion; no action required."
Billing Bot: CM-2026-0811 created, queued for next EOD post. Ticket FIN-3299 updated with partner ops assignment.
Evan Cho: Also recommend adding a daily promo audit check to prevent future backdating re: reseller sync. Nina can you raise a quick Ops ticket?
Nina Soto: On it  creating OPS-1063 to add promo validity enforcement in the partner-sync pipeline.
Jordan Blake: Thanks team. I'll send the customer note once CM posts. Closing loop after posting confirmed. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-platform: 1777777777
preview:
Alex Kim: Heads-up: saw a spike in audit-export billing for tenant-42 after the last rollout. suspect telemetry tags missing actor pseudonymization.
Maria Chen: :eyes: can you share the sample payload?
Alex Kim: Paste: ```POST /v1/audit/exports 200
Headers: X-Audit-Tenant: tenant-42
         X-Audit-Actor: user:alex@redwood.com
Body: ndjson of change events
```
Sam Patel: ah — we currently tag X-Audit-Actor with full email when run via console. that's the cause. we intended to pseudonymize for downloads >= presigned duration.
Jordan Blake: That explains the billing + GDPR note. We should not change behavior mid-rollout. Need migration plan.
Liz Torres: Suggestion: add telemetry field "actor_resolution_mode": ["email","pseudonym","service"] in export manifest + console badge.
Maria Chen: Agreed. UI should show (i) actor_level: full/pseudonymized, (ii) retention label.
Sam Patel: Implementation question: do we attach actor identity to every exported line or just add a header mapping file? header mapping preferred to keep NDJSON stable.
Alex Kim: header mapping means consumers can rehydrate; but risk leaking in S3 logs. maybe encrypt mapping with KMS.
Jordan Blake: +1 KMS. Also quota gating: if tenant enabled full-actor, count against privileged-change quota.
OpsBot: deploybot: canary deploy queued for audit-export-service v2 at 11:30 PT :rocket: 
Liz Torres: For telemetry: emit event `audit.export.start` with tags: tenant_id, actor_mode, export_size_estimate, route=console|api. keep sample rate 100% for privileged_mode.
Maria Chen: Also add `audit.export.actor_resolution` histogram for latency of actor lookup to catch slow LDAP lookups.
Sam Patel: I'll prototype a minimal header schema: ```X-Audit-Mapping: s3://redwood-exports/tenant-42/actor_map.json.enc``` and include `X-Audit-Actor-Mode: pseudonym`
Alex Kim: Please add migration note in console rollout: default to pseudonym unless tenant opts in for full. schedule deprecation of old behavior in 6 weeks.
Jordan Blake: filing RFC for rollout + quota changes. tagging @maria and @sam for review.
Maria Chen: ack, will take a look today.
Liz Torres: ok, I'll add console badge copy and make the tooltip explain KMS.
Alex Kim: Thanks — we'll sync tomorrow after the RFC. :thumbsup:

title: #product: 1772001234
preview:
Marta Ruiz: Quick sync — product decision needed on convo API shape. Customer asks: some want simple `prompt` param, others expect `messages` array with roles (system/user/assistant). We need launch criteria and scope. Thoughts?
Mike Chen: my 2c: canonical API should be `messages` array. It's explicit, maps to chat models and tool calling. Keep a `prompt` alias in SDKs for ease but server canonicalizes to `messages`.
Priya Patel: Agree. Engineering concerns: telemetry for role-level token counts, model routing decisions, and legacy clients that send a single string. We'll need automatic conversion + warn header. Also impacts batching.
Jordan Blake: Devex ask: docs + migration guides first-class. Example snippets, quickstart & playground support. Also update TS SDK types and python.
Samira Ortiz: Role semantics matter — system should be immutable and high-trust. Recommend server-side enforcement: single `system` messages allowed at top, merges with preconfigured system prompts. That avoids customer confusion.
Marta Ruiz: Good. What about function/tool calling + structured output? Does `messages` cover it?
Mike Chen: Yes. We'll extend `messages` items with `name`/`function_call` hints. Example:
```
messages: [
  {role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant."},
  {role: "user", content: "Lookup user orders"},
]
```
and responses stream ops unchanged.
Priya Patel: Launch criteria proposal:
- server canonicalizes `prompt` -> `messages`
- SDKs expose both shapes with deprecation warnings
- docs + migration guide
- telemetry: role token metrics + failure rates
- E2E tests with function-calling flows
Jordan Blake: Also need playground + codegen updates and sample datasets for evals.
Marta Ruiz: Priority: M1 (launch) = canonical messages + `prompt` alias + docs + SDK updates. M2 = stricter system enforcement + role-level policy settings + analytics dashboards. M3 = remove prompt alias (TBD by usage).
deploy-bot: scheduled deploy to staging for canary testing at 2025-12-15T09:00Z :rocket:
Marta Ruiz: OK. I'll draft RFC based on this and gate on telemetry threshold (degradation <1%) + docs ready. Thanks all :thumbsup:

title: #design: 1771234567
preview:
Alex Chen: Quick design review thread — chart legend + outlier handling for the Metrics dashboard. Figma here: https://www.figma.com/file/PRIVATE/metrics-legends (private). Main points: legend interactions, toggling behavior, clipping spikes. Thoughts?

Maria Gomez: Love the compact legend but two UX concerns: toggling series currently reflows and autos-cales the y-axis which is confusing. Also big spikes from cold-starts dominate the view.

Samir Patel: +1 on preserving scale. Proposal: when user toggles a series, keep the current y-axis unless they have 'autoscale' enabled. Also add a 'Cap outliers' toggle that clips at the `99th percentile` and marks clipped points with a caret marker.

Jordan Blake: Accessibility notes: legend items must be keyboard-focusable (tab / enter to toggle). ARIA live region should announce "Series X hidden" / "shown". Use colorblind-safe palette and add shape markers for lines so toggles aren't only color-dependent.

Alex Chen: Copy suggestion: change label from 'Clip spikes' to 'Cap outliers (99th pct)'. Tooltip when clipped should show both displayed value and original: `Throughput: 3.1k (clipped from 12.8k)`. Keep tooltip copy concise.

Samir Patel: For the clipping behavior UX: when clipping is on, show a subtle gradient at the top of the chart and a tiny ^ marker on clipped points. Clicking the ^ expands a mini-popover showing the original point history. Also add a short help text: "Capped at 99th percentile to reduce visual skew."

Maria Gomez: Layout note: legend goes multi-column after 4 items, and on mobile it becomes a collapsed 'Series' dropdown with checkboxes. Avoid horizontal scrolling. On hover show full series name with `title`/tooltip.

Jordan Blake: Smoothing: don't enable by default. Add an opt-in control: `Smoothing: off | 7-point rolling | 30-point rolling`. If smoothing is on, optionally show a faint dashed raw line under the smoothed line so engineers can see noise.

DesignBot: Prototype updated in Figma: https://www.figma.com/proto/PRIVATE/metrics-proto — added 'Cap outliers' toggle, legend keyboard interactions, and multi-column legend. Staging preview: https://staging.redwood.com/metrics?proto=legend-cap

Alex Chen: Quick accessibility checklist item: ensure legend toggles have `aria-pressed` state, the cap toggle has `aria-checked`, and any clipped-marker popover is reachable via keyboard.

Samir Patel: Tooltip copy for clipped points I'll add in the PR: `value: {displayed} (original: {original}) — capped at 99th percentile`. Will link PR when ready (wip #4321).

Maria Gomez: I'll update the Figma frames for the multi-column legend + mobile dropdown and adjust microcopy to 'Cap outliers (99th pct)'.

Jordan Blake: Scheduling a quick usability session Friday 5pm — 6 participants (SRE, Product, two engineers). Tasks: toggle series, enable cap outliers, read clipped tooltip value, tab through legend, switch smoothing. Will share recording.

Alex Chen: :thumbsup: Action items: Maria — figma updates; Samir — tooltip + PR; Jordan — accessibility checklist + user test; Alex — consolidate specs and add to design doc. Thanks everyone.

Maria Gomez: noted, will do.

Samir Patel: added to my checklist, PR branch `metrics/legend-cap`.

Jordan Blake: I'll send calendar invite for Friday.

DesignBot: Snapshot deployed to staging: https://staging.redwood.com/snapshots/metrics-legend-cap-2026-03-18
