title: #product: 1770243102
preview:
Chris Osei: flagging a caveat for the Q1 pricing exp readout: meter fixes overlap w/ the experiment window. we need to be explicit in the doc + in the charts or we’re going to argue about conversion/margin deltas that are just metering.

Chris Osei: can we align on the segmentation rule? i’m proposing: pre-meter-fix vs post-meter-fix slices, plus exclude a small rollout buffer window.

Nikhil Sharma: yep. Variant B started 2026-01-22 for self-serve new signups. Meter changes started landing 2/06-ish (retry double-count fix + usage event idempotency tightening + region dimension backfill).

Nikhil Sharma: If we aggregate all of Jan+Feb we’re mixing apples/oranges.

Sana Farid: +1. From stats side, mixing measurement changes into outcome metrics breaks the causal story. Need a clean “measurement regime” dimension.

Sana Farid: Suggest we do:
- Primary analysis: only post-meter-fix (stable meter) and only users first exposed after the fix
- Secondary: full window but include meter_version controls + note discontinuity

Logan Wright: meter side: we have at least 3 separate “gotchas”:
1) ENG-4430 / PR-27463: fixed double-counting on server retry/fallback for streaming. That will reduce tokens billed for some traffic.
2) PR-27555: idempotency/dedupe improvements. That reduces duplicate usage events in certain partial failure paths.
3) PR-27541 + backfill: region dimension was missing for some events → those were getting defaulted in downstream agg. Backfill changes historical breakdowns (and some pricing if region-based multiplier applies).

Logan Wright: also PR-27518 shadow billing compare started earlier, but it wasn’t customer-facing. The “real invoice meter” changed when we flipped the gate (Finance-approved). We need to anchor on that date.

Nadia Rahman: concretely: “meter_version” for hosted tokens is `hosted_tokens_v1` vs `hosted_tokens_v1_1` (retry+streaming patch) + `v1_2` (dedupe key change). it’s not a public field but we can derive from the internal usage_events schema version + deploy tag.

Nadia Rahman: for analysis, easiest is to use `usage_event_schema_version` + `billing_calc_version` from the recon export (Snowflake). That already captures the flip.

Rishi Malhotra: for FP&A model + margin readout: we need to avoid interpreting lower “tokens billed” as “customers used less”. It’s just we stopped double counting.

Rishi Malhotra: So guardrail: any ARPU / GM charts must show both:
- raw tokens (actual work done)
- billed tokens (invoice-facing)
…and annotate when billed token definition changed.

Chris Osei: ok so for the readout:
1) add an “Instrumentation / Metering Changes” slide w/ a timeline
2) for key outcomes (conversion, retention, ARPU, GM): show pre/post and highlight discontinuity
3) when we do cohort-based retention: define cohorts by *exposure date* AND *meter regime*

Jordan Blake: for GTM: can we put a crisp disclaimer in the exec summary? like “meter fixes materially affect billed token volume starting Feb 6; conclusions about unit economics are based on post-fix data.” sales is going to ask “why did margin jump overnight?”

Aisha Bello: we should also include the alert/incident context. there was an elevated duplicate rate in late Jan during the backfill attempt, and we tuned it down after. that could create a weird spike if you’re looking at daily usage.

Aisha Bello: I can pull the exact date range for the duplicate-rate anomaly + recommend exclusion window (e.g., 2026-01-28 to 2026-01-30).

Sana Farid: segmentation proposal (writing it out so we can paste into the doc):
- Define `meter_regime`:
  A) Regime A (pre-fix): before invoice meter flip
  B) Regime B (transition): deploy/backfill window (exclude from primary)
  C) Regime C (post-fix): after flip + after backfill completion
- Primary experiment readout uses only Regime C users + exposures
- Sensitivity analysis: include Regime A but report separately; do NOT pool

Nikhil Sharma: what are we calling the “flip” date? I have Feb 6 in my head but want the actual timestamp.

Logan Wright: Finance-approved invoice-facing flip for hosted meter was 2026-02-06 21:00 PT (approx). Dedupe hardening was 2/08.

Logan Wright: Backfill for missing region ran 2/09 into 2/10. It rewrote historical partitions for some accounts.

Nadia Rahman: + the embeddings batch meter fix (PR-27569) landed 2/12. Not directly part of Variant B (mostly chat), but any “API total spend” metric will be affected for accounts heavy on embeddings.

Chris Osei: got it. We’ll define Regime B as 2/06–2/12 inclusive for “all-product spend” metrics, and tighter windows for chat-only.

Rishi Malhotra: please also ensure the stop/continue decision thread references this. We shouldn’t stop an experiment because KPI noise spiked during meter changes.

Evelyn Hart: agree. add it to the readout + to the stop-criteria doc as a formal caveat: “measurement change freeze” window. if metering changes, either pause evaluation or segment.

Evelyn Hart: Action items:
- Chris: add slide + methodology note in readout draft
- Logan/Nadia: send exact flip/backfill timestamps + query snippet to derive meter_regime
- Aisha: list anomaly windows from data quality alerts

Chris Osei: :thumbsup: will do. if folks are ok, i’ll add a bold callout near the top: “Do not compare Variant B vs Control across the meter transition window without segmentation.”

Jordan Blake: +1. also add a one-liner we can reuse for sales enablement (“we improved metering accuracy; some accounts will see billed token counts decrease starting Feb 6, without a change in usage”).

Nikhil Sharma: one more nuance: experiment assignment for existing users happened on plan change events; those can occur before Feb 6 but usage after Feb 6. for retention, we should key on usage dates not assignment dates.

Sana Farid: yes. For behavioral metrics, time index should be “days since first billable usage under experiment pricing,” not “days since assigned.” Otherwise we’re smearing the transition.

Chris Osei: cool. i’ll incorporate. thanks all.
text:
Chris Osei: flagging a caveat for the Q1 pricing exp readout: meter fixes overlap w/ the experiment window. we need to be explicit in the doc + in the charts or we’re going to argue about conversion/margin deltas that are just metering.

Chris Osei: can we align on the segmentation rule? i’m proposing: pre-meter-fix vs post-meter-fix slices, plus exclude a small rollout buffer window.

Nikhil Sharma: yep. Variant B started 2026-01-22 for self-serve new signups. Meter changes started landing 2/06-ish (retry double-count fix + usage event idempotency tightening + region dimension backfill).

Nikhil Sharma: If we aggregate all of Jan+Feb we’re mixing apples/oranges.

Sana Farid: +1. From stats side, mixing measurement changes into outcome metrics breaks the causal story. Need a clean “measurement regime” dimension.

Sana Farid: Suggest we do:
- Primary analysis: only post-meter-fix (stable meter) and only users first exposed after the fix
- Secondary: full window but include meter_version controls + note discontinuity

Logan Wright: meter side: we have at least 3 separate “gotchas”:
1) ENG-4430 / PR-27463: fixed double-counting on server retry/fallback for streaming. That will reduce tokens billed for some traffic.
2) PR-27555: idempotency/dedupe improvements. That reduces duplicate usage events in certain partial failure paths.
3) PR-27541 + backfill: region dimension was missing for some events → those were getting defaulted in downstream agg. Backfill changes historical breakdowns (and some pricing if region-based multiplier applies).

Logan Wright: also PR-27518 shadow billing compare started earlier, but it wasn’t customer-facing. The “real invoice meter” changed when we flipped the gate (Finance-approved). We need to anchor on that date.

Nadia Rahman: concretely: “meter_version” for hosted tokens is `hosted_tokens_v1` vs `hosted_tokens_v1_1` (retry+streaming patch) + `v1_2` (dedupe key change). it’s not a public field but we can derive from the internal usage_events schema version + deploy tag.

Nadia Rahman: for analysis, easiest is to use `usage_event_schema_version` + `billing_calc_version` from the recon export (Snowflake). That already captures the flip.

Rishi Malhotra: for FP&A model + margin readout: we need to avoid interpreting lower “tokens billed” as “customers used less”. It’s just we stopped double counting.

Rishi Malhotra: So guardrail: any ARPU / GM charts must show both:
- raw tokens (actual work done)
- billed tokens (invoice-facing)
…and annotate when billed token definition changed.

Chris Osei: ok so for the readout:
1) add an “Instrumentation / Metering Changes” slide w/ a timeline
2) for key outcomes (conversion, retention, ARPU, GM): show pre/post and highlight discontinuity
3) when we do cohort-based retention: define cohorts by *exposure date* AND *meter regime*

Jordan Blake: for GTM: can we put a crisp disclaimer in the exec summary? like “meter fixes materially affect billed token volume starting Feb 6; conclusions about unit economics are based on post-fix data.” sales is going to ask “why did margin jump overnight?”

Aisha Bello: we should also include the alert/incident context. there was an elevated duplicate rate in late Jan during the backfill attempt, and we tuned it down after. that could create a weird spike if you’re looking at daily usage.

Aisha Bello: I can pull the exact date range for the duplicate-rate anomaly + recommend exclusion window (e.g., 2026-01-28 to 2026-01-30).

Sana Farid: segmentation proposal (writing it out so we can paste into the doc):
- Define `meter_regime`:
  A) Regime A (pre-fix): before invoice meter flip
  B) Regime B (transition): deploy/backfill window (exclude from primary)
  C) Regime C (post-fix): after flip + after backfill completion
- Primary experiment readout uses only Regime C users + exposures
- Sensitivity analysis: include Regime A but report separately; do NOT pool

Nikhil Sharma: what are we calling the “flip” date? I have Feb 6 in my head but want the actual timestamp.

Logan Wright: Finance-approved invoice-facing flip for hosted meter was 2026-02-06 21:00 PT (approx). Dedupe hardening was 2/08.

Logan Wright: Backfill for missing region ran 2/09 into 2/10. It rewrote historical partitions for some accounts.

Nadia Rahman: + the embeddings batch meter fix (PR-27569) landed 2/12. Not directly part of Variant B (mostly chat), but any “API total spend” metric will be affected for accounts heavy on embeddings.

Chris Osei: got it. We’ll define Regime B as 2/06–2/12 inclusive for “all-product spend” metrics, and tighter windows for chat-only.

Rishi Malhotra: please also ensure the stop/continue decision thread references this. We shouldn’t stop an experiment because KPI noise spiked during meter changes.

Evelyn Hart: agree. add it to the readout + to the stop-criteria doc as a formal caveat: “measurement change freeze” window. if metering changes, either pause evaluation or segment.

Evelyn Hart: Action items:
- Chris: add slide + methodology note in readout draft
- Logan/Nadia: send exact flip/backfill timestamps + query snippet to derive meter_regime
- Aisha: list anomaly windows from data quality alerts

Chris Osei: :thumbsup: will do. if folks are ok, i’ll add a bold callout near the top: “Do not compare Variant B vs Control across the meter transition window without segmentation.”

Jordan Blake: +1. also add a one-liner we can reuse for sales enablement (“we improved metering accuracy; some accounts will see billed token counts decrease starting Feb 6, without a change in usage”).

Nikhil Sharma: one more nuance: experiment assignment for existing users happened on plan change events; those can occur before Feb 6 but usage after Feb 6. for retention, we should key on usage dates not assignment dates.

Sana Farid: yes. For behavioral metrics, time index should be “days since first billable usage under experiment pricing,” not “days since assigned.” Otherwise we’re smearing the transition.

Chris Osei: cool. i’ll incorporate. thanks all.

title: #sales: 1770219944
preview:
Jordan Blake: Need quick escalation + approval on a discount/credit exception for an enterprise deal. @Sofia Mendes @Michael Grant @Laura Bennett @Rishi Malhotra looping you in.

Jordan Blake: Account: ZenChat (procurement stage). They’re asking for an exception to our standard usage pricing for Hosted API.
- Term: 12 months
- Commit: $300k annual usage commit (prepay)
- Ask: 22% discount off list for overage rate + $30k one-time credits to cover ramp
- Rationale: they’re spooked by token-metering changes/news + want “price protection” while they validate cost.
- Close date: end of next week. Redlines on usage definition are still open.

Maya Alvarez: +1 context: ZenChat wants credits applied to “any usage including embeddings + rerank.” Also asked if credits can extend beyond term if unused (I said no, pending approval).

Soojin Lee: Flagging: this overlaps w/ the Q1 packaging experiment window. We should be careful to not create a one-off that breaks guardrails / creates a new anchor.

Chris Osei: Do we know if they’re in any experiment cohort right now (Variant B)? If yes, we should exclude from experiment readouts or tag as “manual exception.”

Maya Alvarez: They’re sales-led, not self-serve. I don’t think they’re in Variant B, but I’ll confirm in HubSpot.

Jordan Blake: Here’s the HubSpot link: https://hubspot.redwoodinference.internal/company/zenchat (internal). Notes are in “Pricing & Legal.”

Rishi Malhotra: Finance questions:
1) What’s the expected monthly burn profile? (helps margin / revrec timing)
2) Any Dedicated component or purely Hosted API?
3) Is the $30k credit a true “make-good” or marketing incentive? (accounting treatment)

Maya Alvarez: Burn: they’re projecting ~20–25M tokens/day after month 2, plus embeddings batches for search. No Dedicated this year, purely Hosted API.

Laura Bennett: 22% off list + $30k credits on a $300k commit is effectively >30% concession depending on burn. What’s the competitive pressure? Are we matching someone?

Jordan Blake: Competitive: they’re comparing to “build on own GPUs” + another inference vendor offering 25% off + big credits, but no Private deployment option. They care about audit logs + retention + VPC later.

Emily Stone: RevRec angle: if this is a prepay commit, credits that can be applied to the same performance obligation need to be clearly defined in the order form (and not open-ended). Also: no “credits roll over past term” unless we want a mess.

Sofia Mendes: Legal questions:
- Are we offering any special dispute/refund language tied to metering changes?
- Is this a standard order form or are we letting them customize usage definitions?
If they’re asking for “price protection,” procurement often tries to sneak in retroactive adjustments.

Michael Grant: +1. If we approve anything, we should keep it in the commercial construct (discount + capped credits), not “we’ll re-rate invoices if meter definitions change.” That becomes an auditability nightmare.

Nikhil Sharma: Product note: with the metering hardening initiative, we can point to “billed_tokens” + cache-hit attribution improvements, but we should avoid contractually guaranteeing a specific token-counting implementation beyond the definitions.

Jordan Blake: They did ask for: “If token counting changes materially, customer can dispute and receive retroactive credits.” I told them we can discuss a bounded dispute window + standard process, but not open-ended retro.

Sofia Mendes: That’s the right answer. We can offer our standard dispute window + audit trail. No retroactive “meter change true-up” language.

Chris Osei: For experiment hygiene, please add a note in HubSpot: “manual pricing exception approved” + tag for exclusion. If we don’t, this will pollute conversion + ARPU.

Maya Alvarez: Will do.

Rishi Malhotra: Proposed Finance counter (to fit guardrails):
- Discount: cap at 15% off list overage rate
- Credits: up to $15k, must be used within first 90 days, applies to Hosted API only (exclude Dedicated, if added later)
- No extension, no cash value
This keeps effective concession closer to ~20% max.

Laura Bennett: Agree with Rishi’s structure. Jordan/Maya—does that still close?

Jordan Blake: Might be tight. They explicitly asked for 20%+ to “feel safe.” Can we flex on one dimension?

Emily Stone: If we flex, I’d rather flex discount than credits. Credits are harder operationally (invoice application timing, customer confusion, support tickets).

Michael Grant: +1. Credits also get negotiated endlessly. Discount is cleaner if the usage definition is tight.

Sofia Mendes: Also if we do credits, we must specify: applies to “Usage Charges” only, not taxes/fees, and only after invoice issuance. And we need a clear expiration date.

Jordan Blake: Ok. How about:
- 18% discount on overage
- $10k credits, expiring end of month 3
- $300k prepay commit unchanged

Rishi Malhotra: 18% + $10k is acceptable from margin perspective given no Dedicated and expected ramp. But I want confirmation they won’t push for retro true-ups tied to metering.

Sofia Mendes: I can take the redline. We’ll keep standard usage definition language + standard dispute window. Any “meter change” clause gets struck.

Michael Grant: Add: “Redwood may update its measurement systems; billing will be based on the Usage Metrics as defined herein.” Plus dispute window (e.g., 30 days) and sole remedy as credit on a future invoice, at our discretion.

Laura Bennett: Approving the 18% + $10k credits (3-month expiry) contingent on:
1) No retroactive re-rating clause
2) Credits do not extend beyond term / no cash value
3) ZenChat excluded/tagged from pricing experiment analysis

Jordan Blake: :thumbsup: Thanks. @Sofia Mendes / @Michael Grant can you confirm once the order form language is clean? We’ll hold the line on the “meter change” ask.

Sofia Mendes: Yep. I’ll update the redline today and send to the deal room. Will loop @Emily Stone before we send.

Maya Alvarez: Updating HubSpot now with the approved structure + experiment exclusion tag.

Chris Osei: 🙏 please also note “approval source: Laura + Rishi + Legal” so we can audit later.

Jordan Blake: Done. Appreciate the fast turnaround.

title: #product: 1770197742
preview:
Chris Osei: dropped the readout draft here for quick eyes before we send to Evelyn/Jordan. doc: https://drive.google.com/.../pricing-experiment-readout-slide-draft (comment mode on). main Q: do we recommend continue Variant B ramp or pause?

Nikhil Sharma: skimming now. overall narrative is good. first nit: slide 2 headline says “+6% conversion” but the chart shows +6% *trial->paid* for new self-serve only. call out the funnel stage explicitly.

Soojin Lee: +1. also slide 1 “packaging improvements” is vague. if we’re keeping this internal, can say “free tier + overage guardrail messaging / thresholds” so sales doesn’t interpret as a public announcement.

Jordan Blake: reading w/ sales brain: slide 7 objections section is helpful but we need one clear sentence on billed vs raw tokens + cache hits. right now it’s buried in speaker notes.

Chris Osei: got it. I’ll pull that up into the slide body.

Rishi Malhotra: finance notes:
- slide 5 margin chart: please add the assumption block (GPU cost per 1M tokens, cache hit rate, model mix baseline). otherwise this will get challenged.
- also label whether margin is “contribution margin proxy” vs actual GM (since Dedicated costs aren’t in this analysis).

Emily Stone: revrec/accounting nit: anywhere we mention “invoice impact” we should say “expected invoice impact (subject to reconciliation / meter version)” — don’t want this read as final invoicing guidance.

Logan Wright: telemetry caveat: we *must* pin the meter_version on the charts. the window overlaps the region dimension fix + retry dedupe patch. right now slide 3 says “Jan 15–Feb 15 clean” which is not true.

Chris Osei: yeah, I was worried about that. what’s the right wording?

Logan Wright: suggest:
- split graphs into pre/post Feb 6 (meter fixes rollout) or at least add a vertical line + annotation.
- add footnote: “Usage + billed_tokens derived from usage_events vX; Feb 6 introduced idempotency + region dimension patch; comparisons across boundary are directional only.”

Aisha Bello: +1 and please don’t use the combined time series for the stop/continue decision. for decision, use the last 7 days post-fix where drift alert is green. i can paste the drift status screenshot into appendix if helpful.

Sana Farid: stats notes:
- slide 4: show confidence intervals (or at least p-value / Bayesian probability of lift) for conversion + retention. “+6%” without uncertainty will trigger bike-shedding.
- cohort definition needs to be on-slide, not just in appendix. current sample excludes “accounts with billing disputes” which can bias conversion upward (support-heavy users are filtered out). call that out.
- do we have stop criteria results? (support ticket rate / dispute rate) would be good as a guardrail panel.

Jordan Blake: yes please. the CRO question will be: “did disputes go up?” even if we caveat meters.

Chris Osei: we have Support ticket rate by cohort in the appendix right now. I can move to main deck.

Soojin Lee: on charts: slide 6 has two line colors that are basically identical (control vs treatment). pls update palette. also y-axis starts at 0? right now it’s truncated and looks scarier.

Nikhil Sharma: decision framing: i’d recommend “continue at current ramp (25%) for 1 more week post-meter-fix, then re-readout” vs “ramp to 50% now”. the readout doesn’t justify expanding yet.

Rishi Malhotra: agree. we don’t have stable enough margin read yet. also if we ramp while meter fixes are still settling, finance will reject conclusions.

Emily Stone: +1, and if we are changing pricing thresholds, we should align with the month-end usage freeze window. please call out “no meter changes during ramp” + “invoice preview daily for exposed cohort”.

Logan Wright: small correction: meter changes are frozen but backfills can still happen. we should say “no material metering logic changes; backfills tracked and annotated.”

Aisha Bello: I can add an “incident/backfill annotations” row in the dashboard and link it in appendix.

Elliot Price: for the written takeaway slide, can we add a “customer-facing messaging readiness” line? e.g., “Not ready to externalize (docs/Console copy pending Legal review); internal enablement only.” helps align with PM-1860.

Jordan Blake: +1. sales enablement needs a clean line: “if asked, here’s the explanation” — but we shouldn’t imply we’re running an experiment unless legal okays.

Soojin Lee: exactly. also, the readout says “customers prefer predictable bills” which is true, but we should cite something (support tickets / call quotes). otherwise it reads like vibes.

Chris Osei: i can add 2 bullets w/ quotes from the Acme/Northwind calls (no names, just “enterprise prospect” / “self-serve customer”).

Sana Farid: one more: retention metric is “7d active” but experiment window is short. please label it “early activation / 7d repeat usage” not “retention” to avoid over-claiming.

Nikhil Sharma: good catch.

Chris Osei: ok consolidating edits:
1) clarify funnel stage + add uncertainty
2) add explicit meter_version + pre/post segmentation + vertical line Feb 6
3) margin assumption box + label as proxy
4) move guardrail panel (support/disputes) to main deck
5) adjust chart colors + y-axis
6) revise recommendation to “hold at 25% for 1 more week post-fix”
7) add customer quote snippets + messaging readiness

Anything else blocking sending to execs?

Rishi Malhotra: not blocking, but please add “next data pull date” + “who signs off” (Finance + Eng).

Logan Wright: +1 include owner list for post-fix readout (Chris/Sana/Rishi/Logan) so it’s clear.

Jordan Blake: add one slide note: “no GTM rollout until sales FAQ updated + Legal wording approved.” keeps us out of trouble.

Chris Osei: done. I’ll incorporate and re-post v2 in ~2 hours.

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: ping me before you finalize; I’ll sanity check the drift panel / annotations.

Sana Farid: :eyes: I’ll drop suggested CI wording in comments on slide 4.

Nikhil Sharma: thanks all. once v2 is out, I’ll forward to Evelyn w/ the recommendation + caveat about meter boundary.

Chris Osei: cool. moving thread to resolved once v2 link is up.

4) move guardrail panel (support/disputes) to main deck
5) adjust chart colors + y-axis
6) revise recommendation to “hold at 25% for 1 more week post-fix”
7) add customer quote snippets + messaging readiness

Anything else blocking sending to execs?

Rishi Malhotra: not blocking, but please add “next data pull date” + “who signs off” (Finance + Eng).

Logan Wright: +1 include owner list for post-fix readout (Chris/Sana/Rishi/Logan) so it’s clear.

Jordan Blake: add one slide note: “no GTM rollout until sales FAQ updated + Legal wording approved.” keeps us out of trouble.

Chris Osei: done. I’ll incorporate and re-post v2 in ~2 hours.

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: ping me before you finalize; I’ll sanity check the drift panel / annotations.

Sana Farid: :eyes: I’ll drop suggested CI wording in comments on slide 4.

Nikhil Sharma: thanks all. once v2 is out, I’ll forward to Evelyn w/ the recommendation + caveat about meter boundary.

Chris Osei: cool. moving thread to resolved once v2 link is up.

title: #product: 1770171022
preview:
Chris Osei: Dropping a thread for Packaging Variants v3 open Qs ahead of exec review. Please reply w/ answers or “needs decision” so I can roll into the doc.

Chris Osei: Link to v3 draft (working) in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood/product-ops/packaging-variants-v3-working-draft (same as last week, new tab “Open Questions”).

Nikhil Sharma: +1. Biggest thing: we need to be explicit on what’s “billed tokens” vs “raw tokens” in the packaging copy. Meter fixes are changing some semantics.

Soojin Lee: Adding from PMM side: Sales keeps asking if we can say “cache hits are free” or “cache hits are discounted” and what the customer sees in usage exports.

Chris Osei: Cool. Here’s the Q list (please tag w/ #1 etc):
1) Free tier: do we hard-cap requests/tokens or allow overage at list?
2) Overage behavior: do we soft limit w/ alerts + allow, or block (HTTP 402/quota) when included usage is exceeded?
3) Plan limits: are limits per workspace, per API key, or per org? Any per-user concept?
4) Enterprise exceptions: do we allow custom overage rates, or only custom “included” bundles?
5) Model mix: do we keep a single blended token rate across models in a plan, or per-model multipliers?
6) Caching: how do we attribute tokens for cache hits in the bundle (0 billed? partial billed?)
7) Retries/fallback: do we ever bill for server-side retries? (and how do we message it)
8) Billing granularity: daily vs monthly included usage burn-down (what do we show in Console)
9) Credits: do promo credits apply to overage only, included only, or both?
10) Dispute window / refunds: do we need packaging-specific language or just MSA standard?

Jordan Blake: Re #2 (overage behavior): for self-serve we should not hard block unless fraud/abuse. Blocking causes churn + support load. Prefer soft limit + alert + “upgrade now” in Console.

Emily Stone: Finance angle: soft limit is fine but we need deterministic invoice outcome. If soft limit = unlimited, we need clear overage rates and no “surprise” usage definitions. Also month-end close freeze window — late events can cause “included” to shift unless we define a cutoff.

Rishi Malhotra: For #5 model mix: blended rate makes forecasting easy but can nuke margin if customers camp on large models. Suggest blended for Starter only; Pro/Business should be “base rate + per-model adders” or we enforce model gating by plan.

Nikhil Sharma: Agree. Also we’re about to expose `billed_tokens` vs `raw_tokens` + `cache_hit` in Usage API. If we do blended, it should key off billed tokens only.

Soojin Lee: Wait — how do we explain per-model adders without sounding like “gotcha pricing”? Our current positioning is “predictable.”

Jordan Blake: We can say “plan includes access to X models; premium models priced separately” (like add-on). AEs can sell it.

Sana Farid: On #1 free tier: experiments show hard caps reduce abuse but also lower activation for legit devs. If we keep hard caps, we should set them high enough + add a “verify email + add card” step to extend.

Chris Osei: Can someone propose actual numbers for free tier cap (tokens + requests) for v3? Even if placeholders.

Rishi Malhotra: Placeholder proposal (needs exec): Free = 1M tokens/mo + 5k requests/mo, no overage (hard cap). Starter = 20M tokens/mo included + overage at list. Pro = 100M tokens/mo included + lower overage.

Jordan Blake: 1M tokens/mo free w/ no overage feels too stingy for AI-native devs. Also “no overage” = hard stop mid-demo.

Nikhil Sharma: Another option: Free includes small amount + allow overage only after card added (no invoice until threshold). But that’s more product work.

Emily Stone: If overage is allowed on Free with no card, we take on collections risk and revrec complexity. If card required for overage, cleaner.

Soojin Lee: Can we phrase it as “free credits” rather than “included tokens”? Then it’s less weird to require card to continue.

Chris Osei: Logging as decision point: Free tier = credits framing + card required for overage.

Sofia Mendes: Re #4 enterprise exceptions: legal/contracting preference is to keep a small set of constructs.
- OK: custom included bundle, custom committed spend, custom discount % on list.
- Risky: custom definitions of “token” / caching / retries per customer.
If we allow custom overage rates, we need a standard order form template w/ guardrails + clear “usage definition is per Redwood policy.”

Michael Grant: +1. Also for #10 dispute/refunds: I’d rather centralize in usage terms playbook. Packaging page can reference the standard dispute window + “good faith” investigation, but don’t invent plan-specific promises.

Jordan Blake: For #4, Sales will push for “custom overage” on big logos. But fine if we constrain it to discount off published overage rate.

Rishi Malhotra: That’s basically a discount. Works for margin modeling as long as we have approval matrix.

Chris Osei: Great, so #4 answer: enterprise can do discount off list, not bespoke rate card.

Nikhil Sharma: Re #6 caching: we need a clear rule:
- If cache hit returns previously computed tokens, we shouldn’t bill compute again.
But do we bill *any* tokens for the response? (some vendors bill “served tokens” even from cache).

Emily Stone: Billing from a revrec angle: either is fine as long as consistent + disclosed. BUT if we say “cache hits are free,” we need to ensure meter integrity (no double count) and be prepared for customer audits.

Soojin Lee: PMM preference: “Cache hits are discounted (or not billed) — shown separately in usage exports.” If we can show it in Console, it becomes a value prop.

Nikhil Sharma: Technically we can attribute `cache_hit=true` and set `billed_tokens=0` for the cached portion. Question is whether we want “partial” when prefix cached but completion generated.

Chris Osei: Can we align on: prefix cache hit => prefix tokens billed = 0; generated completion billed normally.

Nikhil Sharma: That’s how the implementation is trending. Need to confirm edge cases (streaming + tool calls) but directionally yes.

Sana Farid: If we switch cache billing mid-experiment window, it’ll bias outcomes. We should annotate “pre/post cache meter fix” in analysis.

Chris Osei: Noted (I’ll add a caveat slide).

Nikhil Sharma: Re #7 retries/fallback: policy should be “no double billing for server-side retries/fallback.” We should bill only once per idempotency key / request_id.

Jordan Blake: Please also make sure Support/AEs can say that in plain English.

Michael Grant: Wording: “You will not be charged more than once for the same request due to Redwood-initiated retries or fallback.” BUT leave carve-out for customer-initiated retries / new requests.

Emily Stone: + add “except where you submit a new request” so we’re not on the hook for client replays.

Soojin Lee: We should add a FAQ entry: “If my client retries after timeout, am I billed twice?” Answer: if you reuse idempotency key, we dedupe; if not, it’s a new request. (Engineering please confirm.)

Nikhil Sharma: Yes, idempotency key is the mechanism. We need to ensure SDKs default to it.

Chris Osei: Re #8 burn-down granularity: what’s the plan? daily included usage remaining?

Rishi Malhotra: For Finance, monthly is the invoice unit. For customers, daily burn-down + forecast is better UX.

Jordan Blake: Customers ask for daily exports all the time. Having daily burn-down in Console will reduce “surprise overage” escalation.

Emily Stone: As long as it’s labeled “estimated” until close. We should define a “usage data freshness” SLA.

Chris Osei: OK: show daily burn-down in Console, but make it clear it may lag; invoice based on month-end finalized ledger.

Sofia Mendes: Make sure any “estimate” language is consistent with contract. Don’t promise real-time.

Chris Osei: Re #9 credits: do credits apply to both included + overage? I assume credits offset overage only?

Emily Stone: Typically credits offset invoice total. If plan has included usage, invoice starts at $0 for included; overage is billable; credits can apply to remaining balance. But we should avoid “credits increasing included allowance” because then we have to track weird carryover semantics.

Rishi Malhotra: +1. Modeling assumes credits reduce cash/rev, not expand included. Simpler: credits apply post-metering to charges.

Jordan Blake: Works for Sales as long as we can position “credits reduce your bill.”

Chris Osei: Summarizing tentative answers for the doc:
- #1 Free tier: credits framing; hard cap; card required to continue/overage (decision needed on exact thresholds).
- #2 Overage: soft limit for paid plans (alerts + allow), no hard block except abuse.
- #3 Limits: per org/workspace; API key is not a billing boundary.
- #4 Enterprise exceptions: allowed = discount off published rates / custom included bundle; not allowed = bespoke usage definitions.
- #5 Model mix: blended for low tier; gating/add-ons or per-model multipliers for higher tiers (decision needed).
- #6 Caching: cached prefix billed_tokens=0; generated tokens billed; usage export shows cache_hit.
- #7 Retries/fallback: never double-bill for Redwood-initiated retries; idempotency key required for client retries.
- #8 Burn-down: daily estimate in Console; month-end finalized for invoice.
- #9 Credits: apply to charges post-metering (primarily overage), not as “extra included.”
- #10 Dispute/refunds: reference standard usage terms; no plan-specific promises.

Evelyn Hart: This is good. Two decisions for exec review: (a) free tier cap + card gating, (b) model gating vs blended. Can you tee those up as explicit options w/ pros/cons and margin impact?

Rishi Malhotra: I can add margin deltas for blended vs gating (esp on large model usage). Will post in the working draft in ~2 hrs.

Soojin Lee: I’ll draft customer-facing phrasing for caching + retries that doesn’t scare people. Will paste here for review.

Michael Grant: Send me the final copy before it goes to Sales enablement. Need to avoid absolute guarantees.

Chris Osei: Perfect. I’ll update the “Open Questions” tab and prep the exec pre-read. :thumbsup:

title: #sales: 1770145503
preview:
Maya Ortiz: hey folks — quick customer-facing Q. how do i explain “billed tokens” vs “raw tokens” in a way that doesn’t sound like we’re hiding the ball? customer is looking at their app-side token counts vs our usage export.

Maya Ortiz: also how do i describe caching? they saw a day where their prompt tokens were huge but the invoice was lower and they’re suspicious.

Jordan Blake: following. this comes up weekly. would love a crisp 2–3 sentence answer + a longer version for the follow-up email.

Soojin Lee: +1. we should standardize the language. Maya, is this for a self-serve customer or in procurement mode?

Maya Ortiz: sales-led renewal convo (they’re technical). they use our OpenAI-compatible endpoint + streaming.

Nikhil Sharma: ok, here’s the framing that’s been working:
- “Raw tokens” = the tokens we *observe* for the request/response using our tokenizer (prompt + completion). It’s what you’d expect if you added up what was sent/received.
- “Billed tokens” = the tokens we charge for after applying billing rules that prevent double-charging (retries/idempotency) and account for cache hits.

Nikhil Sharma: key point: we show raw for transparency, but invoices are based on billed.

Nadia Rahman: adding a bit more detail (customer-safe):
- Streaming: we do NOT bill per chunk. We bill once per request based on the final aggregated token counts.
- Retries/fallback: if our platform retries internally (e.g., transient infra issue, fallback routing), we only bill the successful completion once. Failed attempts are not billed.

Nadia Rahman: caching language (keep it simple):
- When prompt caching is enabled and the request shares a prefix with previous prompts, the model can reuse cached KV state.
- Those cached prefix tokens may be billed at a reduced rate (or not billed depending on plan/config), which is why you can see raw prompt tokens high but billed tokens lower.

Cole Summers: +1 and important nuance: “raw” isn’t what the GPU necessarily computed in full if cache hit; it’s the logical token count of the text. that’s why raw is stable for auditability, and billed reflects the economic lever.

Priya Desai: from Support perspective, we’ve used this one-liner:
“Raw tokens reflect the size of your inputs/outputs; billed tokens reflect what you’re charged after platform optimizations (like cache reuse) and safeguards (like not double-counting retries).”

Maya Ortiz: this is good. do we have an example i can paste?

Nikhil Sharma: example you can use (numbers illustrative):
“Example: You send a 10,000-token prompt where the first 8,000 tokens match a previously cached prefix. Raw prompt tokens will still show ~10,000 for that request, but billed prompt tokens may reflect only the non-cached portion (or discounted cached portion), so your invoice can be lower while output quality stays the same.”

Jordan Blake: can we avoid committing to “not billed” vs “discounted” in writing? depends on plan right?

Nadia Rahman: yes. recommended phrasing:
- “cache-hit tokens may be discounted” (safe)
- “your invoice uses billed_tokens, which reflect cache discounts where applicable”

Nadia Rahman: also if they ask “why doesn’t my client-side tokenizer match exactly?” we can say:
- tokenizers differ by model/version
- we use the tokenizer associated with the model deployed in Redwood
- we expose the counts we use for billing in the usage API/export

Cole Summers: and if they’re comparing against OpenAI usage objects: we’re aligning semantics on the compat endpoints. the near-term answer: “use Redwood’s billed_tokens field for invoice reconciliation.”

Soojin Lee: here’s a customer-ready blurb (short):
“Redwood reports both raw and billed token counts. Raw tokens are the observed prompt/completion token totals for transparency. Billed tokens are what your invoice is based on after applying billing rules (e.g., not double-counting retries) and any cache-hit discounts when prompt caching is in effect.”

Soojin Lee: longer follow-up (email-friendly):
“Raw tokens reflect the logical size of the text sent and generated. Billed tokens reflect how Redwood charges for a request after applying platform safeguards and optimizations: (1) internal retries/failovers are deduped so you aren’t charged multiple times for the same request, (2) streaming responses are billed once based on the final aggregated output, and (3) when prompt caching is enabled, tokens served from cache may be discounted because they don’t require full recomputation. For invoice reconciliation, please rely on the billed token fields in the Redwood usage export/API, which match invoiced usage.”

Maya Ortiz: perfect. last Q: if they ask “how can i verify cache is happening?”

Cole Summers: safest answer today: “we expose cache-hit attribution in the usage export/API fields (rolling out) and can provide a short export for a time window.” Don’t promise exact per-request cache math beyond what we publish.

Nikhil Sharma: + we can offer: “We can compare a before/after window once caching is enabled and show the billed-vs-raw gap increasing as cache hit rate increases.”

Jordan Blake: cool. Maya, use Soojin’s blurb + the example. if procurement asks for contract language, route to Legal playbook.

Maya Ortiz: got it. thanks all — sending now.

Priya Desai: if they come back with “we think we’re double charged”, tag Support + we’ll pull the recon export. (we’ve had a few retry-related false positives recently but we have fixes landing.)

Soojin Lee: longer follow-up (email-friendly):
“Raw tokens reflect the logical size of the text sent and generated. Billed tokens reflect how Redwood charges for a request after applying platform safeguards and optimizations: (1) internal retries/failovers are deduped so you aren’t charged multiple times for the same request, (2) streaming responses are billed once based on the final aggregated output, and (3) when prompt caching is enabled, tokens served from cache may be discounted because they don’t require full recomputation. For invoice reconciliation, please rely on the billed token fields in the Redwood usage export/API, which match invoiced usage.”

Maya Ortiz: perfect. last Q: if they ask “how can i verify cache is happening?”

Cole Summers: safest answer today: “we expose cache-hit attribution in the usage export/API fields (rolling out) and can provide a short export for a time window.” Don’t promise exact per-request cache math beyond what we publish.

Nikhil Sharma: + we can offer: “We can compare a before/after window once caching is enabled and show the billed-vs-raw gap increasing as cache hit rate increases.”

Jordan Blake: cool. Maya, use Soojin’s blurb + the example. if procurement asks for contract language, route to Legal playbook.

Maya Ortiz: got it. thanks all — sending now.

Priya Desai: if they come back with “we think we’re double charged”, tag Support + we’ll pull the recon export. (we’ve had a few retry-related false positives recently but we have fixes landing.)

title: #product: 1770132280
preview:
Chris Osei: Heads up: the Variant B pricing experiment is getting *really* noisy after this week’s meter fixes. We need a decision today: stop vs continue (or pause enrollment) so we don’t publish a misleading readout. Current dashboard is showing a step-change in billed_tokens on Feb 12–13 and conversion looks like it “improved” overnight.

Nikhil Sharma: Yeah, I’m looking at it now. The step-change lines up exactly with the usage event idempotency + streaming retry fix rollout. It’s not a user behavior change.

Sana Farid: +1. If the measurement changed mid-flight, the causal story is broken unless we segment pre/post and re-baseline. Otherwise the lift is basically “we stopped double counting.”

Rishi Malhotra: From Finance lens, we can’t use this to justify packaging decisions if the unit economics inputs moved mid-experiment. Margin model uses billed_tokens and cache_hit attribution — both are shifting.

Chris Osei: The open question: do we (a) stop Variant B entirely, (b) keep running but freeze new exposure + analyze only clean window, or (c) extend experiment with a fresh ramp once meters stabilized.

Logan Wright: Quick context: Feb 12 rollout included:
- fix for double-counting on retry/fallback (esp streaming)
- dedupe/idempotency improvements
- some backfilled missing fields for cache_hit on a subset
So the “billed_tokens” time series discontinuity is expected. It should be *more correct* now.

Nadia Rahman: Important nuance: it’s more correct, but it’s not comparable to the earlier window. Also for a few hours we had partial deployment by region, so aggregates are extra weird. If you do anything, segment by `meter_version` (old/new) + region.

Jordan Blake: From GTM: we promised a decision for AE guidance early next week. If we stop now, we’ll be short on sample for enterprise-ish accounts. But I’d rather be late than ship a story customers will pick apart.

Evelyn Hart: What’s our stop criteria in the experiment doc? Did we already hit any hard guardrails (revenue leakage / churn signals)?

Chris Osei: Guardrails are fine. No spike in refunds/credits requests. The issue is statistical/measurement: conversion rate and ARPU are unstable because the denominator moved.

Sana Farid: If we want a defensible readout, we need:
1) freeze enrollment while meters settle (avoid mixing)
2) define “clean” measurement window post-fix (e.g., after full rollout + after late events catch up)
3) re-run power calc / expected time to detect
We can still keep existing cohort in their assigned plan to avoid customer thrash.

Nikhil Sharma: Agree. Pausing enrollment is easy (flag off for new). Keeping existing exposure avoids changing pricing on people mid-month.

Monica Patel: FYI SDK retry behavior changed slightly too (we’re aligning idempotency key usage in the TS client). That might affect request counts for a small subset if they were doing aggressive client retries. Another reason not to compare pre/post.

Rishi Malhotra: Also revrec: if we’re changing meters, we need clean documentation on the window used for analysis vs billing, otherwise we’ll get questions later when metrics don’t match invoice previews.

Chris Osei: Ok but what’s the decision? “Continue” vs “stop” is what leadership asked me to summarize.

Evelyn Hart: Proposal: continue experiment intent, but treat it as a restart. Pause new enrollments immediately, then restart enrollment once we have 72h of stable metering + consistent region coverage. Publish readout with explicit “pre-meter-fix data excluded.”

Jordan Blake: Works for me as long as we have a date. When do we expect stability?

Logan Wright: If no more rollouts, we can call it stable once:
- drift checks vs billing ledger are <0.5% for 3 consecutive days
- duplicate rate steady
- late event lag back under SLO
We can probably hit that by mid next week.

Nadia Rahman: + we should annotate the dashboard with a “meter fix boundary” so no one screenshots the step-change as a result.

Sana Farid: If we “restart,” we should also reset the analysis plan: primary metric is conversion-to-paid within 7 days; secondary ARPU at day-14; and margin overlays need the new billed_tokens definition. I can update the stop/continue memo.

Chris Osei: Concrete plan:
- Today: pause new exposure for Variant B (keep existing users on their assigned plan)
- Define clean window start = once meter_sanity dashboard green for 3 days
- Re-ramp enrollment next week, run for 2 weeks post-clean window
- Readout includes only post-clean window + cohorts tagged with meter_version=new
Does anyone object?

Rishi Malhotra: No objection. Please add a note that Finance will only sign off on conclusions using post-fix data and include the reconciliation export link.

Jordan Blake: No objection. Please send a quick note I can forward to Sales so they stop asking “why did ARPU jump yesterday.”

Nikhil Sharma: I’ll flip the experiment flag for new signups and leave existing assignments untouched. Chris, ping me the exact flag name you want paused (self-serve only vs all).

Chris Osei: Pause self-serve new first; keep sales-led accounts as-is for now (we’ll review individually). I’ll post in #sales-enablement too.

Evelyn Hart: Decision recorded: *pause new enrollment, continue as restart post-meter-stabilization*. Chris/Sana: update the readout doc with the caveat and new timeline.

Sana Farid: On it. I’ll also add a short section on why pre/post mixing invalidates the diff-in-diff assumption. (Keeping it non-academic.)

Nadia Rahman: Please make sure analysis uses `billed_tokens` not `raw_tokens` and that cache_hit attribution is consistent. We’ve seen folks accidentally mix those in Looker.

Logan Wright: I’ll drop a one-liner query snippet for filtering on `meter_version` + the rollout boundary timestamps. Also will add an annotation request to the dashboard PR.

Chris Osei: Great. Action items:
- Nikhil: pause self-serve enrollment (today)
- Logan/Nadia: confirm “clean window” criteria + boundary timestamps
- Sana: updated analysis plan + stop criteria re-baseline
- Chris: comms to Sales + exec update blurb

Jordan Blake: :thumbsup: thanks all. Just keep me looped on the new “decision date” so I can reset expectations.

title: #support: 1770083321
preview:
Zendesk: New ticket linked: SUP-28463 ("Double-charged for retries")

Talia Benson: Flagging in here — customer is reporting they were double-charged due to retries/fallback. Ticket SUP-28463. They attached a CSV with `request_id`s where they see 2x billed_tokens vs their own tokenization. Asking for (1) confirmation if we had a bug, (2) whether their Feb invoice needs a credit, (3) how to prevent.

Talia Benson: Customer notes it happens mostly on streaming chat completions + occasional 502s. They suspect client retry, but they’re saying they only retry on network errors and they set idempotency keys.

Cole Summers: Thanks. If they’re setting idempotency keys and still seeing 2x, that points to our server-side retry/fallback path counting twice (usage event emission + retry event emission). We had an open bug in that area.

Logan Wright: Yeah, this matches ENG-4430 / PR-27463 work (“fix double-counting on stream retry”). We saw cases where a partial stream emitted usage, then fallback kicked in and we emitted usage again, both landing in ledger.

Nadia Rahman: +1. The repro we had was: stream chunk(s) delivered -> upstream timeout -> gateway retries to same model variant (or fallback) -> second usage event has new `attempt_id` but same logical request. If dedupe key is wrong (attempt-scoped vs request-scoped), billed_tokens double.

Emily Stone: Do we know timeframe? Was this active during Feb invoice period and did we already close/issue invoices? Need to know whether this is “forward fix only” or requires credit for a sent invoice.

Talia Benson: Ticket says invoice received yesterday (so likely already issued). They’re asking for a breakdown by day.

Cole Summers: I can pull an estimate if we have their account id + date range. Do we have the account name in the ticket?

Talia Benson: Yep — Acme AI. They cited Feb 1–Feb 4 spikes, then it “looks normal” after.

Logan Wright: That lines up with the rollout. I think we shipped the stream retry fix Feb 5 morning UTC to hosted.

Nadia Rahman: We should confirm via shadow-billing compare if possible. But for support response we can likely say: we identified an issue where server-side retries could cause double-counted billed tokens for a small subset of streaming requests; fix deployed Feb 5; we can compute impacted requests and issue credit.

Emily Stone: Please be explicit: credit (not retroactive invoice edit) unless CFO/Legal says otherwise. For Acme, we can issue a credit memo / apply to next invoice. Also, make sure we don’t say “we overbilled everyone” — keep scoped.

Jordan Blake: +1 on wording. Customer-facing: acknowledge discrepancy, explain retry semantics, and note that idempotency keys protect client retries but this was on our server retry path. Offer credit + export of impacted request_ids.

Cole Summers: I’ll run a quick query on duplicates for Acme. Looking for same `request_id` with multiple usage events where `retry_reason` is non-null / fallback set.

Cole Summers: (sharing query sketch)
```sql
select
  date_trunc('day', event_ts) as day,
  count(*) as usage_events,
  count(distinct request_id) as distinct_requests,
  sum(billed_tokens) as billed_tokens,
  sum(case when attempt_number > 1 then billed_tokens else 0 end) as retry_attempt_tokens
from billing.usage_events
where account_name = 'Acme AI'
  and event_ts >= '2026-02-01'
  and event_ts <  '2026-02-06'
  and endpoint = 'chat.completions'
  and streaming = true
group by 1
order by 1;
```

Logan Wright: If you can also join ledger to see whether both attempts landed as billable line items, that’ll help Finance.

Cole Summers: Yep. Give me ~20.

Nadia Rahman: Also check dedupe window. In the old pipeline the dedupe key was `(account_id, request_id, attempt_id)` which would not collapse retries. New logic should be `(account_id, idempotency_key OR request_id)` with attempt ignored.

Talia Benson: For the ticket response: do we want to request any headers from the customer (idempotency key, request_id) or can we proceed with our internal request ids?

Logan Wright: We can proceed. If they sent request_ids, we can validate against logs.

Cole Summers: Results (rough): Feb 1–4 shows elevated “multiple attempts per request_id” for streaming.
- Feb 1: ~0.9% of streaming requests have >1 attempt; incremental billed_tokens attributable to attempt>1 ~1.8% of billed_tokens that day
- Feb 2: ~1.1% requests; incremental tokens ~2.3%
- Feb 3: ~0.8% requests; incremental tokens ~1.5%
- Feb 4: ~0.6% requests; incremental tokens ~1.0%
After Feb 5: drops to baseline (<0.05%).

Emily Stone: Great. Can you translate that into a dollar estimate for credit? Even a range is fine so we can approve quickly.

Cole Summers: Will do. I’ll compute using their contracted per-1K token rates. (Need to confirm whether they’re on experiment variant pricing or standard.)

Jordan Blake: Acme might be on a pricing experiment (Variant B) — check before quoting amounts.

Talia Benson: Ticket doesn’t mention pricing experiment. I’ll check HubSpot after this, but for now we can say “we’ll calculate and apply a credit once confirmed” rather than quote.

Cole Summers: Updated: using their current ledger rates, estimated overbill for Feb 1–4 is ~$842 (most from Feb 2). I’ll attach the breakdown in the ticket notes for Finance sign-off.

Emily Stone: Approved to credit, assuming no additional scope. Please log the calculation + query output in the ticket for audit trail. We’ll apply as a credit memo to next invoice (preferred) unless customer insists.

Logan Wright: From Eng side: fix is in prod; PR-27463 shipped; also additional idempotency hardening is in PR-27555 (in review) to prevent similar duplicates under partial failures.

Nadia Rahman: Also note: the customer may still see two *attempts* in logs/usage exports, but billed_tokens should reflect a single logical request post-fix. If they’re consuming raw usage events, they need the new billed_tokens field in usage API (PR-27485) to avoid confusion.

Talia Benson: Drafting customer response. Proposed:
1) confirm we identified an issue where server-side retry/fallback on streaming could cause double-counting of billed tokens for a small subset of requests
2) fixed Feb 5
3) we calculated impact for their account Feb 1–4 and will apply a credit (~$842) to next invoice (or alternative if they need)
4) reassure: client idempotency keys are still recommended; this was not due to their client retries
5) offer a list of impacted request_ids + daily breakdown

Jordan Blake: Wording tweak: don’t mention internal PRs. Add: “no action required on your side” + “we can provide a one-time reconciliation export” + point them to the updated usage definition doc once public.

Emily Stone: Also add: “If you have internal chargeback or month-end close needs, we can provide a dated credit letter.”

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: And please avoid saying “bug” — say “issue” / “metering discrepancy”.

Talia Benson: Got it. Posting response in SUP-28463 and looping Finance for credit memo. Will update thread if customer pushes for invoice re-issue.

Cole Summers: I’ll drop the impacted request_id list in the ticket as an attachment and note the filter criteria so we can reproduce.

Nadia Rahman: If customer asks why their usage API didn’t match invoice: explain that usage API previously surfaced raw attempt-level tokens in some views; we’re adding explicit `billed_tokens` + `cache_hit` fields for clarity. 

Talia Benson: Sent. Thanks all.

title: #product: 1770050444
preview:
Chris Osei: rolling up a rollout checklist for pricing experiment Variant B. want to run it by folks before we flip anything. can we confirm: eligibility, flags, dashboards, comms?

Nikhil Sharma: yes pls. Variant B is the “new free tier + overage guardrail copy” right? + tweak to overage rate for self-serve?

Chris Osei: yep. Variant A was copy-only, Variant B is copy + packaging thresholds (included usage + overage behavior).

Sana Farid: reminder: if meters changed last week, we need explicit pre/post segmentation in the analysis. for rollout checklist: record meter_version and experiment cohort on every request.

Logan Wright: +1. from telemetry side we can tag `meter_version` + `pricing_experiment_variant`. PR-27533 adds cohort tagging but we need to ensure it’s wired on OpenAI-compat + SDK retries.

Monica Patel: SDK side: python/ts will pass idempotency keys by default on retries, but older clients may not. for the experiment cohort tagging, can we do it server-side based on account_id so it’s not client-dependent?

Nikhil Sharma: cohort assignment should be server-side (control plane) based on account + workspace. client should be unaware.

Chris Osei: ok draft checklist items (pls correct):
1) Eligibility:
- self-serve only (no sales-led contracts)
- Hosted API only (exclude Dedicated + Private)
- exclude accounts with custom rate cards / invoice terms
- exclude accounts with open billing disputes (Support tag)
- geo: start US/EU only?
2) Flags:
- LaunchDarkly: `pricing_exp_2026q1` + `pricing_exp_variant=B`
- ramp: 0% -> 5% (24h) -> 25% (48h) -> 50% if stable
- ensure holdout/control remains 50%
3) Telemetry:
- events: `pricing_plan_exposed`, `plan_id`, `variant`, `cohort_id`
- attach to usage events + billing ledger export (Finance recon)
4) Dashboards:
- conversion funnel, ARPU, gross margin proxy, churn/retention
- guardrail: support tickets, dispute rate, “usage export mismatch” cases
5) Comms:
- Sales/Support FAQ snippet + internal heads-up
- finance: invoice preview note for impacted accounts

Soojin Lee: on comms: if we change overage behavior, we need consistent copy in Console + docs + emails. otherwise Support gets crushed.

Jordan Blake: sales request: clear “what changed / who is affected / how to answer fairness questions”. esp billed vs raw tokens + caching.

Rishi Malhotra: finance request: for the ramp steps, can we get an “invoice preview” report daily for impacted cohort? need to validate revenue/margin deltas aren’t coming from meter drift.

Aisha Bello: dashboards: I can add an annotation stream when ramp changes (5%/25%/50%) so we can correlate with metrics + alerts. but need the exact flag key + timestamp.

Ethan Park: eligibility can be enforced at control plane: include rule “account.plan_type == SELF_SERVE” and “account.billing_profile == STANDARD”. we can also block if `billing_hold=true`.

Chris Osei: love it. quick questions:
- US/EU only: do we need region constraint for pricing? or just ensure region dimension is correct for margin attribution.

Logan Wright: pricing calc shouldn’t be region dependent but margin model is. we had the “missing region dimension” incident recently. i’d prefer we limit to accounts where region is stable + the region dimension is present (null-rate < threshold) for the first ramp.

Sana Farid: +1. for experimental validity: excluding accounts with missing region may bias, but for rollout safety it’s fine. just document that exclusion.

Nikhil Sharma: ok, include a “data quality eligibility”: only accounts with >= 99.5% non-null `region` on usage events in last 7d.

Chris Osei: adding.

Soojin Lee: also: console surfaces “included tokens” and “overage starts at”. need to ensure the UI strings are behind the same flag, otherwise ppl see mismatch vs invoice.

Monica Patel: and API/SDK usage endpoint needs to show the same fields (billed_tokens + cache_hit) so customers can reconcile. is PR-27485 already in prod?

Logan Wright: PR-27485 is staged, rolling out to prod this week. but experiment B shouldn’t depend on it being customer-visible—just helps support.

Support Bot: FYI Support queue label `billing-usage-discrepancy` currently at 14 open tickets (last 7d). Top issue: “invoice tokens don’t match usage export”.

Jordan Blake: that’s exactly why we need comms in advance. don’t want the experiment to look like it increased disputes when it’s just confusion.

Rishi Malhotra: on ramp: can we add stop criteria: dispute rate > X%, or gross margin delta outside expected band, or meter drift alerts firing.

Sana Farid: propose stop criteria for first 48h:
- dispute/support tickets in exposed cohort > control by +30% (normalized by active accounts)
- conversion drop > 10% vs control (new signups)
- revenue per active account change > +/-20% without corresponding usage shift
- meter drift alert triggered (ledger vs usage events) beyond threshold

Aisha Bello: we can pipe the drift alert signal into the experiment dashboard as a red banner.

Nikhil Sharma: ok.

Chris Osei: flag + cohort details: do we have final naming? I see “VariantB” in the doc but we should standardize.

Ethan Park: we can make it `pricing_exp_2026q1.variant = B` and `pricing_exp_2026q1.cohort = control|treatment`. string values.

LaunchDarkly Bot: Feature flag update: `pricing_exp_2026q1` rules modified by Ethan Park (added targeting attribute `billing_profile == STANDARD`).

Chris Osei: perfect.

Soojin Lee: comms checklist:
- Support macro draft (what changed, where to see usage, how overage is calculated)
- Sales FAQ snippet for objections: “are you counting retries?”, “cache hits billed?”, “streaming partials?”
- Console banner copy for “You’re in a limited pricing test” (internal? or customer-facing?)

Michael Grant isn’t here but legal will likely want us NOT to say “pricing test” to customers unless we’re ready to honor that language. can we keep it internal-only for now?

Nikhil Sharma: agree. no explicit customer banner for Variant B. eligibility is self-serve, but still.

Jordan Blake: then at least internal comms: AEs need to know if a customer asks “why did my included usage change?”

Chris Osei: last part: operational checklist for the actual flip:
- [ ] confirm meter_version is pinned for analysis (pre/post)
- [ ] confirm Snowflake export table available to Finance (ledger recon)
- [ ] dashboard links shared in #product + #finance
- [ ] Support macro published
- [ ] Sales enablement FAQ updated
- [ ] set ramp schedule + owner on-call

who’s on point during ramp day 1?

Nikhil Sharma: I can own product side. Logan for telemetry. Aisha for dashboards/alerts. Rishi for finance check.

Logan Wright: ok. pls give 30 min heads up before 5% so we can watch dupes + nulls.

Aisha Bello: i’ll set alert thresholds lower during the first 2 hours so we catch issues quickly, then revert.

Rishi Malhotra: and please freeze any meter changes during the ramp window. don’t want confounding.

Nikhil Sharma: agreed. change freeze: no metering PRs to prod for 48h after first exposure.

Chris Osei: awesome. i’ll post the checklist as a pinned note in this channel + link the Linear ticket (PM-1848/PM-1860) for traceability.

Jordan Blake: :thumbsup: add me to review the Support/Sales wording before it goes out.

Soojin Lee: same. i’ll draft the short internal blurb + macro today.

Chris Osei: thx all. proceeding with “ready to ramp” once comms docs land + we confirm region null-rate eligibility query.

Logan Wright: i’ll drop the query here later today.

Aisha Bello: will share dashboard link once annotation wiring is in.

title: #finance: 1769998888
preview:
Maya Chen: FYI noticed a token surge in yesterday's ledger after delayed ingest — looks like ~1.21M extra tokens processed at ~02:15 UTC, affecting ~119 invoices. Need owners + timeline to correct invoices.
Carlos Rivera: ugh. saw that. pipeline replayed a late batch from S3 that had duplicate events. I'm happy to own the rollback/reconciliation if Priya + finance confirm scope.
Priya Nair: Thanks Carlos. Confirmed duplicates in metering ingestion (duplicate_message_id). Can you share the filter/query you'll use for dedupe? Also need list of affected accounts and provisional credit amounts for finance.
Carlos Rivera: planning to run de-dup job: `python tools/dedupe_tokens.py --after 2026-02-01T02:00Z --before 2026-02-01T03:00Z --key event_id`
Maya Chen: timeline from finance: need provisional credits & estimate by EOD PST, formal invoice corrections in next billing cycle unless urgent.
Jordan Blake: cc @Sam O'Neill, any risk if we revert the replay? Would that impact other downstream reconciliations?
Sam O'Neill: Reverting replay is safest but will re-run downstream aggregations. I'll coordinate; estimate 3-4 hours for full reprocess in staging, 6-8 hrs for prod if we throttle.
Carlos Rivera: ok I'll prepare the dedupe job and a dry-run in staging at 14:00 UTC. Will post logs + diff.
Priya Nair: please include a sample SQL diff: `select account_id, sum(tokens) from usage where timestamp between ... group by account_id` before/after.
Maya Chen: Owners: Carlos = remediation run, Priya = ops validation, Maya = finance approval/credits. Target: dry-run 14:00 UTC, prod run 18:00 UTC, credits file by EOD PST.
Jordan Blake: note recurring pattern: similar delayed replay in Nov — add postmortem action to alert on S3 replays >1hr lag and to require manual approval for replays >10k events.
Maya Chen: added to postmortem action list: `alerts: delayed_replay_threshold`, owner = eng-infra.
Carlos Rivera: posting staging dry-run results in 14:30 UTC. Will tag this thread with results + a small CSV.
billing-bot: Ingest anomaly detected: duplicate_event_rate=0.032, affected_accounts=123, estimated_overcount_tokens=1213000
Maya Chen: thanks — will prepare provisional credits sheet and share with @Jordan and @Priya. If prod run goes ahead, finance to push invoice correction notes.
Carlos Rivera: dry-run results: dedupe delta_tokens=-1,212,984 (expected) — sample diff attached (CSV). Prepping prod run at 18:15 UTC (shifted 15m).
Sam O'Neill: prod run complete at 21:00 UTC. Reconciliation started; initial pass indicates ~1.21M tokens removed, 119 accounts >$100 impact. No downstream data loss.
Maya Chen: closing note: credits prepared, accounting will queue invoice adjustments next cycle unless customers request immediate reissue. Action items captured in postmortem doc: eng-infra to add alert, infra to add replay gating, finance to own invoice adjustments. Thanks everyone :thumbsup:

title: #product: 1769954321
preview:
Alex Chen: Quick sync — we need a concrete spec for an introspection/discovery endpoint that lists runtime-supported features per model instance (tool schemas, streaming modes, token caps). Customer asks from FinReg want deterministic max token limits and function-calling schemas.
Priya Nair: +1. Proposal: /v1/models/:id/discover returning compact JSON with keys: supported_tools, streaming_modes, max_input_tokens, max_output_tokens, structured_output_schema, function_schemas, version, compatibility. Keep it stable.
Sam Ortiz: Keep payload small and flag-driven. Instead of heavy nested objects, expose top-level capability flags: supports_functions, supports_streaming, streaming_type, supports_json_output, supports_rerank. Put full schemas behind separate endpoints if needed.
Jordan Blake: Also include quantization/embedding caveats — e.g., "embedding_precision": "fp16|int8|approx" to signal deterministic behavior differences.
Maria Gomez: Launch criteria checklist suggestion: contract locked + docs, SDK updated, canary rollout path, perf SLOs validated, legal signoff for token limits for regulated customers.
Alex Chen: Example discovery response (for discussion):
```
{
  "model_id": "redwood-1.4-quant",
  "version": "2026-03-01",
  "capabilities": {
    "supports_streaming": true,
    "streaming_type": "delta",
    "supports_functions": true,
    "supports_structured_output": "json_schema",
    "supports_rerank": "batched",
    "max_input_tokens": 8192,
    "max_output_tokens": 4096,
    "embedding_precision": "int8_approx",
    "supported_tools": ["search","sql-exec"]
  },
  "function_schemas_url": "/v1/models/redwood-1.4-quant/functions",
  "compatibility": {"fallback_models": ["redwood-1.3-std"]},
  "meta": {"cost_estimate_per_1k_output_tokens": "0.012_usd", "p50_latency_ms": "45", "p95_latency_ms": "180"}
}
```
Priya Nair: A few notes: 1) function_schemas_url keeps discovery light-weight; 2) add a TTL header and encourage SDK caching; 3) version the endpoint as /discover?v=1 or /discover/1.
Sam Ortiz: Ops ask: include a signal whether the model enforces strict JSON schema validation vs best-effort post-processing (boolean or enum). Also a deprecation field for soon-to-be-removed features.
Jordan Blake: For streaming, indicate whether tool-calls can stream function arguments (some runtimes block streaming for function call bodies). Field: "function_call_streaming": true/false.
Maria Gomez: Prioritization input: three enterprise pilots require deterministic token ceilings + function schema validation for audit logs. That makes this P0 for Dedicated/Private; for Hosted API we can stage P1 behind perf validation.
Alex Chen: Rollout plan draft: 1) spec and OpenAPI fragment (eng + devex) 2) alpha with 1 partner (dedicated) 3) canary in dedicated pool + SDK flag 4) open-hosted rollout when perf & cost numbers validated. Also add migration docs for SDKs to cache discovery.
deploy-bot: deploy:discovery-schema v0.1 -> staging passed smoke tests :rocket:
Priya Nair: Next steps — I'll open a GH issue with the OpenAPI fragment and owners. Need engineering estimates and a short UX doc for console feature flag.
Sam Ortiz: Rough ETA: spec + tests 3w, implement 4w, docs + SDK updates 1w. Might overlap.
Alex Chen: Thanks, team — sync Thurs 11am to finalize scope and owners. :thumbsup:

title: #announcements: 1769700001
preview:
Chris Osei: :mega: Marketplace Listing Refresh is LIVE (AWS + GCP)

Big thanks to everyone across PMM/Partnerships/Eng/Security/Docs/RevOps. Starting today we’re moving off the legacy marketplace listings and onto the refreshed offers + onboarding flow.

What changed (buyer-facing):
1) New marketplace plan/SKU naming (consistent across AWS + GCP)
   • Hosted API (Usage-based)
   • Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)
   • Private Deployment (VPC)
   Add-ons:
   • Compliance Pack
   • Premium Support

2) Listing content refreshed
   • Clear Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private differentiation
   • Updated screenshots + "How it works" section
   • Procurement-friendly FAQ (billing, entitlement timing, support)

3) Marketplace onboarding hardening (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key)
   • New console marketplace entrypoint
   • Entitlement verification API + improved error states
   • Entitlement sync job retries + alerting

4) Security documentation bundle for marketplace review
   • Marketplace-friendly security overview + shared responsibility
   • Data handling/retention + residency notes
   • Audit logging overview + export options

Where to find enablement (internal):
- PMM copy / positioning blocks (approved language): confluence → Sales Enablement → Positioning: "Marketplace positioning + copy guidelines"
- Procurement one-pager (when to route to marketplace vs direct): confluence → Sales Enablement → Pitch Decks: "Marketplace procurement one-pager"
- Customer-facing FAQ draft (billing, entitlements, trials/credits): drive → GTM → "customer-facing-marketplace-faq-draft"
- Security packet index (what’s public vs gated): confluence → Security & Compliance → "Marketplace security documentation pack"
- Deal ops + required HubSpot fields: drive → RevOps → "revops-process-marketplace-deal-ops"

Escalation / help:
- Provisioning/entitlement issues (stuck linking org, missing entitlement): #support (tag @Dev Patel) + open SUP ticket
- Billing dimension mismatch / SKU mapping questions: #eng-platform (tag @Cole Summers)
- Security questionnaire / evidence requests: #eng-security (tag @Naomi Feldman) + loop in @Olga Petrov if evidence gating needed

If you’re customer-facing this week: please route marketplace buyers to the new onboarding docs + FAQ first. We want to reduce “time-to-first-token” + support load.

Dropping a quick checklist in thread next.

Camila Reyes: +1 and please use the new screenshots/copy only (don’t pull from the old listing pages). If you see outdated assets floating around, ping me or @Soojin Lee.

Soojin Lee: Quick note on language: avoid saying “SOC2 compliant” in emails/decks. Use the approved phrasing from the copy guidelines (e.g., “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA”) + link the security packet index.

Jordan Blake: This is a big unlock for procurement-led deals. Thank you all. AEs: if you have an account that *must* buy via marketplace, please re-check the right plan name before you send them the subscribe link.

Alex Martinez: Docs are live + nav updated. Marketplace onboarding landing pages are now under Docs → “Marketplace”. If you hit any broken redirects or weird 404s, file INT ticket + tag me.

Elliot Price: Also: security packet publication is split (public vs gated). If a buyer asks for evidence (SOC2, pen test attestation, etc.), don’t forward random PDFs. Use the “Security packet index” page and request access via Security/GRC.

Chloe Martin: Onboarding UX notes:
- If buyer already has a Redwood org, the console now prompts to link subscription → existing org (instead of creating a duplicate)
- If entitlement hasn’t propagated yet, they’ll see a “Try again” state with guidance (and the backend retries are more aggressive now)
- If it’s been >30 min, please escalate (we have alerts but want human eyes fast)

Cole Summers: Billing/metering heads-up:
- Hosted dimensions are now buyer-friendly labels (Text generation input/output, Embeddings, Rerank)
- Dedicated includes Reserved GPU hours (+ Usage overage where applicable)
- If you see a line item that looks wrong, grab the marketplace subscription ID + Redwood org ID and tag me in #eng-platform.

Nadia Rahman: Telemetry is in place for the onboarding funnel. We’re tracking:
- entitlement lookup latency
- org linking failures
- API key creation completion
- first successful request
If you want dashboard access, ping me/Ben.

Ben Carter: Baseline conversion metrics are in the GTM drive folder ("marketplace-onboarding-funnel-baseline-metrics"). We’ll post a weekly update after 1 full week of data.

Naomi Feldman: Security escalation guardrails:
- Standard questionnaires: start with the draft response doc + cite the security packet pages
- Anything requesting raw evidence artifacts (reports, detailed diagrams, vuln scan output): loop in Security before sending
- Residency/retention questions: use the marketplace data handling + retention page (it has the current defaults + configurable options)

Dev Patel: Support macros are ready for:
- “Subscription active but no entitlement”
- “Provisioning delayed >30 minutes”
- “Billing dimension question”
We’ll pin them in #support. If an enterprise buyer is blocked during procurement/onboarding, escalate quickly — we want a clean first week.

Stephanie Nguyen: Field note: buyers will ask “what did I buy?” during org linking. The new plan names are much clearer. Please copy/paste exactly:
Hosted API (Usage-based) / Dedicated Capacity (Reserved) / Private Deployment (VPC)

Lukas Meyer: EMEA teams: Private Deployment (VPC) language is landing well with security reviewers. If anyone gets pressure for “on-prem in marketplace” specifically, flag it — we should route those to Private direct contracting.

Chris Osei: Quick internal checklist (customer-facing):
1) Confirm which marketplace (AWS vs GCP) + which plan name
2) Send: onboarding doc link + FAQ + (if needed) security packet index
3) If provisioning is stuck: collect subscription ID + Redwood org ID + timestamp, open SUP ticket, tag @Dev Patel
4) If pricing/billing questions: route to one-pager + RevOps deal ops doc, tag @Isabelle Laurent as needed

Please reply here if you hit any rough edges today so we can capture + fix fast.
