title: #product: 1769612307
preview:
Chris Osei: Need to lock go/no-go criteria for marketplace refresh launch + proposed date. Dropping a draft checklist here — please poke holes.

Target launch: Wed 2026-02-18 10am PT (AWS + GCP listings + docs live + console entrypoint enabled)

GO criteria:
1) Docs: marketplace onboarding pages published + nav/redirects in place (no dead links from AWS/GCP listing)
2) Security packet: marketplace security overview + data handling/retention + audit logs pages reviewed/approved (wording consistent)
3) Entitlements: entitlement sync job reliability in prod (p95 entitlement availability < 15 min, failure rate < 1% rolling 24h) + alerting wired
4) Console onboarding: marketplace entrypoint shipped + error states for delayed entitlements + retry guidance
5) Metering: dimension mapping verified end-to-end (AWS + GCP) in staging with a test subscription; no known mismatch bugs
6) Approvals: PMM + Partnerships sign-off on listing copy; AWS/GCP partner reviewers cleared (no open “blocker” comments)
7) Support readiness: macros + escalation path for “provisioning delay” + “wrong SKU/dimension” ready; oncall coverage confirmed for launch window

NO-GO if: open P0/P1 in entitlement/provisioning/metering, or partner review not cleared.

Can folks confirm ownership + whether these thresholds are sane?

Ben Carter: Criteria list looks right. On (3) — can we define what we’re measuring for “entitlement availability”? Is it “marketplace subscription exists -> our entitlement API returns ACTIVE”? If yes, we need to ensure telemetry is actually in place for that. ENG-22114 shipped events, but dashboards are still WIP.

Chloe Martin: Console onboarding side: entrypoint is behind flag `marketplace_onboarding_enabled`. Error states include:
- “Entitlement not found yet” w/ wait + retry
- “Subscription already linked to another org”
- “Multiple subscriptions found” w/ picker
We’re good to enable at launch, but I’d like 1 more dry run in prod-like staging with delayed entitlement propagation.

Priya Natarajan: For entitlement sync reliability: p95 < 15 min is ambitious but achievable if the sync job queue is healthy. After ENG-22116/PR-19878 we’ve got retries + idempotency and alerts. Suggest adding a guardrail: “no sustained backlog > 500 msgs for > 10 min” (or equivalent) so we don’t get surprised.

Ethan Park: +1. Also, define failure rate as “sync attempts resulting in ERROR” vs “no-op because already current”. We can track ERROR count / total runs. Right now alert threshold is > 20 errors in 10 min (tunable).

Cole Summers: Metering: we fixed the dimension mismatch in staging last week (ref ENG thread). I still want a clean E2E:
- subscribe -> link org -> make 1 hosted call -> verify usage lands in billing pipeline with correct dimension codes + display names
- repeat for dedicated “reserved GPU hours” mock record
We can do hosted easily; dedicated reservation test is more manual.

Nadia Rahman: Telemetry: events are live (`mp_entitlement_lookup_*`, `mp_org_link_*`, `mp_first_key_created`, `mp_first_successful_request`). Dashboard draft exists but not in #product view yet. I can publish a basic Looker/Mode link today so Ben/Chris can monitor launch.

Alex Martinez: Docs status:
- Onboarding pages are merged in docs repo, pending final redirects (INT-771) so the URLs we gave AWS/GCP won’t 404.
- FAQ page ready.
I’m blocked on final URL list confirmation (Elliot + me) + Security sign-off for the security overview wording.

Elliot Price: Redirects: I’ve got the final set ready; waiting on eng to deploy the docs site redirect config. If we can get it out by EOD tomorrow, we’re good.
Also: we should add a GO criteria line: “AWS/GCP listing links validated in incognito + from buyer view” (we had that miss last time).

Naomi Feldman: Security packet: content is good, but we need to be disciplined on claims. No “SOC2 compliant product” phrasing. We can say “Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II report” + link to request process.
For marketplace review, we can publish:
- Security overview (public)
- Data handling + retention summary (public)
- Audit logs capabilities (public)
Evidence (SOC2 report, pen test) remains gated/on request.

Marcus Lin: +1. Also add a GO check: “encryption + KMS wording consistent across listing + docs.” AWS reviewers ding inconsistencies fast.

Soojin Lee: PMM: listing copy is basically locked. Remaining open items are tiny edits from Partnerships (remove a “guaranteed” adjective, swap “SLA-backed” -> “SLA options available” depending on SKU). I can sign off once Steph confirms partner portal constraints.

Stephanie Nguyen: Partnerships: AWS review round 2 is pending. They asked for:
- support contact + escalation path on listing
- clarification on what’s included in Compliance Pack
We’ve responded; expecting turnaround in 1–2 biz days.
GCP: they’re picky about billing dimensions naming + the security link. Once redirects are live, we can finalize submission.

Dev Patel: Support readiness: macros draft is done (“Provisioning delay after marketplace subscription”, “Wrong charges/dimension name confusion”, “Private offer activation timing”). Need final internal escalation contacts (oncall alias + billing alias) before I publish.

Jordan Blake: From exec sponsor POV: I’m fine with Feb 18 if we hit the go/no-go gates by Monday EOD. If partner approvals slip, don’t hero it — ship docs + console flag off, then flip once approvals are in.

Chris Osei: Great feedback. Updates to criteria:
- (3) define entitlement availability = “entitlement verification API returns ACTIVE for subscription”
- add backlog guardrail (Priya)
- add link validation check (Elliot)
- add encryption/KMS wording consistency check (Marcus)

Revised GO checklist (final):
A) Docs: onboarding + FAQ published; redirects shipped; all marketplace links validated in incognito
B) Security: security overview/data handling/audit logs pages approved; no over-claims; encryption/KMS wording consistent across listing + docs
C) Entitlements: p95 entitlement availability < 15 min; ERROR rate < 1% rolling 24h; no sustained backlog; alerts + runbook link posted
D) Console: marketplace entrypoint shipped behind flag; staging dry run includes delayed entitlements + multi-sub edge case
E) Metering: E2E staging test with a marketplace subscription -> usage lands with correct dimensions (AWS+GCP)
F) Approvals: PMM + Partnerships sign-off; AWS/GCP reviewers cleared (no “blocker”)
G) Support: macros published; escalation aliases confirmed; oncall coverage 10am–2pm PT on launch day

Proposed date stays Feb 18 10am PT, pending A–G green by Monday EOD.

Action owners:
- Nadia: telemetry dashboard link today
- Chloe: staging dry run schedule
- Cole: metering E2E test plan + sign-off
- Alex/Elliot: redirects + docs publish
- Naomi/Marcus: security wording final approval
- Steph: partner approval status
- Dev: support macros + alias list

Ben Carter: +1. Once Nadia posts the dashboard link, I’ll add it to the launch brief + set up a lightweight war room channel for the 10–2 PT window.

Nadia Rahman: Dashboard link is live (internal): https://mode.redwood.inference/marketplace/onboarding-funnel — shows entitlement lookup latency + failures, org link failures, first key created, first successful request. I’ll add an alert panel by tomorrow.

Chloe Martin: I can do staging dry run tomorrow 11am PT. I’ll invite Chris, Cole, Alex. Goal: simulate entitlement delay (hold sync job for ~10 min) + ensure UI messaging is clear.

Cole Summers: I’ll join. For metering, I’ll prep a test subscription in staging and validate dimension mapping in the usage export. If anything looks off, it’s immediate NO-GO.

Chris Osei: Perfect. I’ll post a green/yellow/red status snapshot Monday noon PT in #product based on A–G.

Stephanie Nguyen: Quick update: AWS reviewer replied “looks good” but waiting for formal portal status flip. I’ll ping our AWS contact tomorrow AM.

Naomi Feldman: Security wording is approved from my side once Alex updates one sentence in the overview (“may retain prompts…” -> “retention configurable; defaults described below”). Marcus ok?

Marcus Lin: Yep, that edit is the last one I care about. After that, ship.

Dev Patel: Send me the final escalation aliases (billing + eng oncall). I’ll publish macros same day.

Chris Osei: Will do. Thanks all — keeping Feb 18 as target, with the explicit NO-GO gates.

title: #sales: 1769537745
preview:
Markus Klein: anyone have an approved “Redwood Private deployment security appendix” we can attach to an RFP? Helix Pharma is asking for a dedicated section that spells out controls + shared responsibility (VPC/on‑prem). Our current RFP library is mostly Hosted/Dedicated language.

Stephanie Nguyen: don’t think we have a standalone appendix yet. What’s the ask specifically? (network isolation / customer-managed keys / logging / etc)

Markus Klein: yep. they want (1) architecture/data flow summary for Private, (2) boundary of responsibility (Redwood vs customer), (3) access controls (SSO/RBAC), (4) audit logging + export, (5) vuln mgmt/patching expectations, (6) incident response + how we do evidence (SOC2 report under NDA). They explicitly said “appendix preferred vs answers sprinkled throughout.”

Jordan Blake: +1 we’ve hit this a few times lately. would be great to standardize as an addendum/appendix block in the library.

Hannah Schmitt: EMEA deals too. Nordbank’s questionnaire basically assumed Private = “fully customer controlled” and we had to do a bunch of one-off explanations.

Naomi Feldman: agreed we should add a Private appendix section to the RFP response library (not a new product promise). Colin + Fatima: can you draft the technical bullets + boundaries? Olga can sanity check the compliance phrasing.

Colin O'Donnell: can do. For clarity: “Redwood Private” is either (a) customer VPC with private control plane connectivity, or (b) on‑prem package for regulated/air‑gapped. We need to be careful to keep it generic + not overfit to one implementation.

Fatima Noor: I’ll help draft, esp the regulated/on‑prem expectations. We should include the “who patches what” section (customer owns OS/hypervisor if on‑prem; Redwood owns app/runtime components we ship, per contract), but keep it high level.

Olga Petrov: please also include a short “evidence availability” paragraph + link to the evidence request process (NDA required for SOC 2 report). And avoid ISO language beyond “alignment/mapping” per our do-not-say guidance.

Marcus Lin: +1 on boundaries. Also call out: no inbound access by default; any break-glass access is time-bound + customer-approved + logged. For VPC, network paths are constrained to approved endpoints; for on‑prem, customer controls perimeter.

Avery Johnson: what do we say re: “data never leaves region” in Private? I keep getting asked.

Naomi Feldman: we should stick to approved wording: residency depends on selected deployment region + customer-managed infra in Private. Don’t claim “never leaves” without specifics. For VPC/on‑prem, processing occurs in the customer environment, but support/telemetry may be configured/limited—needs the standard caveats.

Camila Reyes: once drafted, please send to me for messaging pass. We should keep it consistent with our security pillars (shared responsibility, isolation, encryption, auditability) + keep it “competitive-safe.”

Markus Klein: timeline: Helix wants this by Thurs EOD. Even a “v0.1” we can attach with disclaimer “subject to contract/SSOW” would help.

Olga Petrov: we can do a v0.1 as long as it’s approved language. No “subject to roadmap” / no features not GA. Add a footer like “This appendix describes current capabilities as of [date].”

Colin O'Donnell: plan: I’ll draft an appendix section today in the RFP library doc with headings:
- Overview (Private = VPC/on‑prem options)
- Data flow + isolation (high level)
- Shared responsibility model (table-ish text)
- Identity & access (SAML SSO, SCIM where applicable, RBAC)
- Logging & audit (events, export options, retention options)
- Encryption & keys (at-rest/in-transit; KMS/HSM options by mode)
- Vulnerability management & patching (responsibilities)
- Incident response & notification (process, contractual)
- Compliance evidence (SOC 2 report request under NDA)

Fatima Noor: I’ll add language on regulated deployments: customer-controlled networking, options to disable/limit telemetry, and operational expectations (change windows, upgrade cadence) without hard commitments.

Stephanie Nguyen: perfect. once it’s in the library, we should also add a HubSpot snippet + point #sales to the canonical link so we don’t fork docs.

Jordan Blake: +1. Also add a note to the Confluence enablement kit index once published.

Marcus Lin: when you draft, ping me for a quick accuracy pass (esp the “break-glass access” wording + audit log fields). (reaction :thumbsup: from Markus Klein)

Naomi Feldman: action items:
- Colin + Fatima draft v0.1 appendix in RFP library today
- Marcus review for technical accuracy
- Olga review for compliance/evidence phrasing
- Camila final messaging pass
- Stephanie to coordinate publishing/linking once approved

Markus Klein: thanks all — I’ll tell Helix we’re sending an approved appendix tomorrow morning and keep them on track. (reaction :white_check_mark: from Stephanie Nguyen)

Markus Klein: anyone have an approved “Redwood Private deployment security appendix” we can attach to an RFP? Helix Pharma is asking for a dedicated section that spells out controls + shared responsibility (VPC/on‑prem). Our current RFP library is mostly Hosted/Dedicated language.

Stephanie Nguyen: don’t think we have a standalone appendix yet. What’s the ask specifically? (network isolation / customer-managed keys / logging / etc)

Markus Klein: yep. they want (1) architecture/data flow summary for Private, (2) boundary of responsibility (Redwood vs customer), (3) access controls (SSO/RBAC), (4) audit logging + export, (5) vuln mgmt/patching expectations, (6) incident response + how we do evidence (SOC2 report under NDA). They explicitly said “appendix preferred vs answers sprinkled throughout.”

Jordan Blake: +1 we’ve hit this a few times lately. would be great to standardize as an addendum/appendix block in the library.

Hannah Schmitt: EMEA deals too. Nordbank’s questionnaire basically assumed Private = “fully customer controlled” and we had to do a bunch of one-off explanations.

Naomi Feldman: agreed we should add a Private appendix section to the RFP response library (not a new product promise). Colin + Fatima: can you draft the technical bullets + boundaries? Olga can sanity check the compliance phrasing.

Colin O'Donnell: can do. For clarity: “Redwood Private” is either (a) customer VPC with private control plane connectivity, or (b) on‑prem package for regulated/air‑gapped. We need to be careful to keep it generic + not overfit to one implementation.

Fatima Noor: I’ll help draft, esp the regulated/on‑prem expectations. We should include the “who patches what” section (customer owns OS/hypervisor if on‑prem; Redwood owns app/runtime components we ship, per contract), but keep it high level.

Olga Petrov: please also include a short “evidence availability” paragraph + link to the evidence request process (NDA required for SOC 2 report). And avoid ISO language beyond “alignment/mapping” per our do-not-say guidance.

Marcus Lin: +1 on boundaries. Also call out: no inbound access by default; any break-glass access is time-bound + customer-approved + logged. For VPC, network paths are constrained to approved endpoints; for on‑prem, customer controls perimeter.

Avery Johnson: what do we say re: “data never leaves region” in Private? I keep getting asked.

Naomi Feldman: we should stick to approved wording: residency depends on selected deployment region + customer-managed infra in Private. Don’t claim “never leaves” without specifics. For VPC/on‑prem, processing occurs in the customer environment, but support/telemetry may be configured/limited—needs the standard caveats.

Camila Reyes: once drafted, please send to me for messaging pass. We should keep it consistent with our security pillars (shared responsibility, isolation, encryption, auditability) + keep it “competitive-safe.”

Markus Klein: timeline: Helix wants this by Thurs EOD. Even a “v0.1” we can attach with disclaimer “subject to contract/SSOW” would help.

Olga Petrov: we can do a v0.1 as long as it’s approved language. No “subject to roadmap” / no features not GA. Add a footer like “This appendix describes current capabilities as of [date].”

Colin O'Donnell: plan: I’ll draft an appendix section today in the RFP library doc with headings:
- Overview (Private = VPC/on‑prem options)
- Data flow + isolation (high level)
- Shared responsibility model (table-ish text)
- Identity & access (SAML SSO, SCIM where applicable, RBAC)
- Logging & audit (events, export options, retention options)
- Encryption & keys (at-rest/in-transit; KMS/HSM options by mode)
- Vulnerability management & patching (responsibilities)
- Incident response & notification (process, contractual)
- Compliance evidence (SOC 2 report request under NDA)

Fatima Noor: I’ll add language on regulated deployments: customer-controlled networking, options to disable/limit telemetry, and operational expectations (change windows, upgrade cadence) without hard commitments.

Stephanie Nguyen: perfect. once it’s in the library, we should also add a HubSpot snippet + point #sales to the canonical link so we don’t fork docs.

Jordan Blake: +1. Also add a note to the Confluence enablement kit index once published.

Marcus Lin: when you draft, ping me for a quick accuracy pass (esp the “break-glass access” wording + audit log fields). (reaction :thumbsup: from Markus Klein)

Naomi Feldman: action items:
- Colin + Fatima draft v0.1 appendix in RFP library today
- Marcus review for technical accuracy
- Olga review for compliance/evidence phrasing
- Camila final messaging pass
- Stephanie to coordinate publishing/linking once approved

Markus Klein: thanks all — I’ll tell Helix we’re sending an approved appendix tomorrow morning and keep them on track. (reaction :white_check_mark: from Stephanie Nguyen)

title: #partnerships: 1769527811
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: Need approvals today/tomorrow on the *standard AWS Private Offer template* + discount guardrails so we can stop reinventing the wheel per deal. Draft doc here: https://drive.google.com/redwood/aws-private-offer-template-v1 (comment mode on). @Jordan Blake @Laura Bennett @Michael Grant @Sofia Mendes can you review + sign off? Also need an explicit “when to escalate” rule for anything outside guardrails.

Chris Osei: +1. This is blocking the marketplace refresh timeline (AWS submissions + enablement). Would love a clear RACI + which fields are “fixed” vs “editable per deal.”

Jordan Blake: Skimmed. Directionally good. My main concern is discount creep + reps promising custom terms. Can we lock in hard guardrails and a clean escalation path? I want this usable by AEs without a 3-day approval chain.

Laura Bennett: Finance view: we can support a standard template if we’re explicit about (1) min annual commit, (2) max discount by segment, (3) payment schedule, and (4) usage true-ups vs prepaid. Right now the doc says “discount up to 25%” with no segmenting.

Sofia Mendes: Legal view: template is fine but we need to be careful with the language “SLA-backed” in the offer doc unless the customer is buying Dedicated/Private with the SLA addendum attached. Also: AWS Private Offers sometimes require mirroring the Marketplace EULA + our Order Form. Let’s ensure the template references the right Redwood terms package.

Michael Grant: Adding: we should not promise data residency or “no training on customer data” beyond what’s in our published security packet / DPA. I see a section “Data handling notes” in the draft—needs standard wording + link to the security docs, not custom claims.

Stephanie Nguyen: Got it. Proposal for guardrails section (open to edits):
- Standard term: 12 months (default), optional 24 months with Finance approval
- Standard payment: annual upfront; net-30 only (no net-60)
- Max discount (list): up to 10% (Self-serve/Hosted heavy), up to 15% (mid-market), up to 20% (enterprise) *only with CRO approval*
- Min annual commit: $60k Hosted, $120k Dedicated, Private is “quote only”
- No custom SLA language in private offer doc; refer to standard SLA addendum if applicable
Would this satisfy Finance/Legal?

Laura Bennett: Close. I’d rather we do:
- Max discount without CRO: 10% (all segments)
- 15% requires CRO + Finance
- 20% requires CRO + Finance + RevRec review (bc it often implies weird structuring)
Also minimums: for Dedicated we should tie to a capacity reservation floor, not just $ amount. But as a guardrail, $120k is OK as long as we call it “minimum annual commitment equivalent.”

Emily Stone: RevRec: please avoid language implying “prepaid credits” unless we’re treating it as usage-based with variable consideration. For private offers we should keep it simple:
- either contract-based fixed fee (Dedicated reservation)
- or usage-based billed through marketplace with a committed spend floor
If template says “credits roll over” etc, it complicates revrec.

Jordan Blake: Agree w/ Laura. Also I want a single sentence in the guardrails: “Any deviation from standard terms requires written approval in #finance + #legal + #partnerships.” Otherwise we’ll get side DMs.

Sofia Mendes: +1. Specific legal edits:
1) Replace “Redwood will provide 99.9% uptime” with “per Redwood SLA” and only if SLA addendum is attached.
2) Remove “Most favored pricing” language entirely (I saw it in an optional clause section).
3) Add: “This Private Offer incorporates the Marketplace terms + Redwood Order Form v2026-01” (or whatever the correct version is).
4) Add a disclaimer that support SLA depends on support tier / SKU.

Michael Grant: I left comments in the doc around the “Security & compliance” section. Suggest we use a safe block:
“Security posture and controls are described in the Redwood Marketplace Security Overview (link). Any additional requirements are addressed via DPA / security addendum.”
Also, remove “HIPAA ready” from any template language.

Isabelle Laurent: Ops question: where does this live + what’s the process for AEs? Ideally: one canonical doc + a checklist + required HubSpot fields. Otherwise we’ll still have inconsistent submissions.

Chris Osei: We can link it from the internal enablement page and add a HubSpot workflow note (Isabelle can own that). For this thread: can we get “approved with changes” today and move the doc to final by EOD tomorrow?

Markus Klein: From SE side: please keep one editable section for “Deployment scope / region / SKU” because customers ask. But agree we shouldn’t add custom security claims. Also add a standard line about entitlement timing (AWS sometimes takes ~15–60 min). Saves us support escalations.

Stephanie Nguyen: Summarizing proposed *guardrails v1* (based on comments):
- Default term: 12 months. 24 months requires Finance approval.
- Payment: annual upfront default; net-30 only. Anything else = Finance + Legal.
- Discounting:
  - <=10%: AE can proceed (no CRO) if minimums met
  - 10–15%: CRO + Finance approval
  - 15–20%: CRO + Finance + RevRec approval
  - >20%: not allowed (must be exception with exec sign-off)
- Minimum annual commitment equivalent:
  - Hosted: $60k
  - Dedicated: $120k (or capacity-reservation floor; follow-up in v1.1)
  - Private: quote only (no public guardrail)
- No custom SLA text; reference standard SLA addendum only when applicable.
- Security language: standard block + link to published security overview; no “HIPAA ready,” no bespoke residency promises.
- Any deviation requires written approval in channel (thread) with tagged approvers.
If everyone’s good, I’ll update the doc and mark as “v1 approved” once we have explicit yeses.

Jordan Blake: Approved pending those edits. :thumbsup: Please add one more bullet: “No free months” (discount only via %). I’ve seen reps try to do 14 months for 12.

Laura Bennett: Approved with Jordan’s “no free months” addition. Also please add “No retroactive credits/refunds via marketplace” unless Finance signs off.

Emily Stone: Approved w/ the revrec-safe wording and no “rollover credits.” If you keep it as “committed spend floor + usage billed through marketplace,” that’s usually fine.

Sofia Mendes: Approved once the SLA + security wording is corrected and the correct terms package is referenced (Order Form version + Marketplace EULA). I’ll review the final text after you incorporate comments.

Michael Grant: Same—approved pending edits. I’ll do a quick legal read once you update.

Isabelle Laurent: Approved from ops side. Once final, I’ll add a HubSpot snippet + checklist link in the deal workflow doc.

Stephanie Nguyen: Great. I’ll update doc now, resolve comments, and post back the final link + “copy/paste block” for AEs. Targeting EOD today for final review, tomorrow morning for enablement rollout.

Chris Osei: Thank you. When final is ready, I’ll link it in the marketplace enablement bundle + ping #sales and #partnerships with the guardrails summary.

Stephanie Nguyen: Updated doc: https://drive.google.com/redwood/aws-private-offer-template-v1-final (renamed “v1-final”). Incorporated:
- No free months
- Discount approval tiers
- Removed MFN clause
- SLA wording now “per Redwood SLA addendum if attached”
- Security block links to marketplace security overview
- Payment + refunds language clarified
@Sofia Mendes @Michael Grant can you confirm final legal wording is OK to roll out?

Sofia Mendes: Reviewed. Looks good. One nit: change “Order Form v2026-01” to “Order Form v2026-01-15” (latest). Otherwise approved.

Michael Grant: Confirmed. Approved. :white_check_mark:

Stephanie Nguyen: Done (updated version string). Calling this approved + rolling out. Thanks all.

title: #sales: 1769411188
preview:
Avery Johnson: Getting the ISO question again in a questionnaire: “Are you ISO 27001 certified?” What’s our exact wording rn? I don’t want to accidentally imply cert.

Hannah Schmitt: +1. Nordbank asked last week and they were super picky about “compliant” vs “aligned”.

Lukas Meyer: Same in EMEA. If we say “ISO-aligned” they then ask for certificate number :grimacing:

Stephanie Nguyen: Can we get a canonical copy block we can paste into RFPs? Also where is the source of truth (claims matrix vs Confluence)?

Olga Petrov: Jumping in. Approved language (please use verbatim unless you get an exception):

Olga Petrov: 1) If asked “Are you ISO 27001 certified?”
Answer: “Redwood Inference is not currently ISO/IEC 27001 certified. We maintain a security program and information security controls that are aligned to widely adopted industry standards, and we can provide our SOC 2 Type II report under NDA as evidence of our control environment.”

Olga Petrov: 2) If asked “Are you ISO 27001 compliant?”
Answer: “We do not claim ISO/IEC 27001 compliance or certification. Upon request, we can discuss how our security controls map to ISO/IEC 27001 themes (e.g., access control, logging/monitoring, vulnerability management, incident response), and we can provide third-party audit evidence (SOC 2 Type II) under NDA.”

Olga Petrov: 3) If they ask for an “ISO attestation letter”
Answer: “We do not provide ISO attestation letters. We can provide our SOC 2 Type II report under NDA and respond to specific control questions.”

Olga Petrov: Hard “do not say”: “ISO compliant”, “ISO certified pending”, “ISO in progress”, “ISO-ready”, “meets ISO 27001 requirements”. Those have all created problems in redlines.

Avery Johnson: This is perfect. Is there a link I can cite internally so the AE team stops remixing it?

Olga Petrov: Yep. Claims matrix row is the source of truth for copy/paste wording:
- Security claims matrix (restricted): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/RI-Security-Claims-Matrix (tab: “Compliance - ISO27001”)
- Confluence guidance (non-restricted): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/security-faq/what-we-do-and-dont-say#iso27001

Daniel Carter: Adding: if a customer insists on ISO 27001 as a hard requirement, route to GRC early. Sometimes they’ll accept SOC 2 Type II + mapping narrative, sometimes not. Don’t try to negotiate language in-email.

Hannah Schmitt: When they ask “aligned” they usually follow with “show me the mapping”. Are we okay to say we can map Annex A controls?

Olga Petrov: Careful on wording. Approved: “we can discuss how our controls map to ISO/IEC 27001 themes” (high-level). If you need an Annex A control-by-control mapping for an RFP, that’s a GRC deliverable and depends on NDA + timeline. Don’t promise it unilaterally.

Lukas Meyer: For EU banks they literally have a table with each Annex A control and want Yes/No. Should we just say “see SOC2 report” for those?

Daniel Carter: For those: respond with the ISO block Olga posted + “available under NDA: SOC 2 Type II report” + offer a follow-up call. If the RFP requires a strict Yes/No per control, flag it as an exception and we’ll provide alternative language where appropriate.

Naomi Feldman: +1 on not implying roadmap. We’ve had a few questionnaires where “aligned” got interpreted as “certification planned this year.” Please avoid any “in progress” / “working toward” phrasing.

Camila Reyes: From messaging side: we can also add a one-liner to reduce friction:
“While we are not ISO 27001 certified, we undergo independent third-party audits (SOC 2 Type II) and maintain a security program designed around common control expectations.”
This is consistent with the Security & Trust pillars doc.

Stephanie Nguyen: Can we get this into the RFP response library + HubSpot snippet so it’s not tribal knowledge?

Olga Petrov: Yes. Please open an INT request (or tag me) if you need a snippet added. The canonical text is what I pasted above.

Jordan Blake: Thanks all. Stephanie—let’s make sure the enablement kit index points to the claims matrix + this exact block. We’re losing time on rework every time.

Avery Johnson: Copying verbatim into the Nordbank response now. Thanks Olga/Daniel.

Hannah Schmitt: :thumbsup: bookmarking that Confluence link.

Lukas Meyer: :eyes: on getting the HubSpot snippet updated. I’ll ping Isabelle separately.

Daniel Carter: One more reminder: SOC 2 report request is NDA-gated. Don’t attach the report directly; use the evidence request playbook link and we’ll fulfill.

Olga Petrov: ^ yep. Evidence request playbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/compliance-evidence/soc2-customer-evidence-request-playbook

title: #sales: 1769380021
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: Starting a running list of marketplace procurement objections/questions I’m hearing this week. Please drop yours + any good canned responses. Goal is to map these into the updated customer-facing Marketplace FAQ + internal enablement one-pager.

Stephanie Nguyen: Top 5 from my calls:
1) “Can we apply our AWS EDP / commits if we buy via Marketplace?”
2) “Can you do a private offer with net terms / annual commit?”
3) “What exactly do we get when we click Subscribe? Do we get an org automatically or do we need SE help?”
4) “We need SOC 2 + encryption/KMS + audit logs. Where’s the packet? Is it public?”
5) “We have multiple AWS accounts / multiple projects. Can we link multiple subscriptions to one Redwood org?”

Markus Klein: +1 on (3). Also getting “What are the support SLAs for Marketplace customers? Is it the same as direct?” and “Do we get a trial/credits through Marketplace or only direct?”

Lukas Meyer: EMEA angle: “We need GCP Marketplace (not AWS). Can you guarantee data residency in EU regions?” and “Can we turn off data retention / set custom retention?”

Isabelle Laurent: Adding ops/process one: “If we buy in Marketplace, does it still count toward our internal spend tracking? How does invoicing look (AWS invoice vs Redwood invoice)?”

Soojin Lee: For mapping to assets, here are the drafts we should point to (I’ll update links once final URLs are locked):
- Customer-facing FAQ draft (GDrive): /shared_drives/go-to-market/customer-facing-marketplace-faq-draft
- Procurement one-pager (Confluence): /sales-enablement/pitch-decks/marketplace-procurement-one-pager
- Security packet index (Confluence): /security-and-compliance/compliance-evidence/marketplace-security-documentation-pack
- Data handling/retention (Confluence): /security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/marketplace-data-handling-and-retention

Jordan Blake: Please make sure we’re consistent on EDP/commits language. Don’t promise “EDP automatically applies” — depends on customer’s AWS agreement. We should say “Marketplace purchases may count toward AWS commitments depending on your AWS contract; confirm with your AWS rep.”

Ben Carter: I can take (3) and (5) and convert into FAQ entries w/ the new onboarding flow language. Need latest from console team on entitlement propagation timing.

Rachel Kim: From onboarding calls: “How long does entitlement take after Subscribe?” We should give a realistic range + troubleshooting steps (and a clear escalation path). Also “We already have a Redwood org. Can we attach the marketplace subscription to it without creating a new org?”

Dev Patel: Support perspective:
- Expect tickets: provisioning delay, org linking confusion, billing dimension mismatch.
- We should add a line: if entitlement hasn’t shown up after X minutes, check Y, then open a ticket with marketplace subscription ID.
- Also clarify who provides L1 support (Redwood) vs what must go to AWS/GCP support.

Stephanie Nguyen: Awesome. I’ll start mapping Qs -> recommended response + asset.

Stephanie Nguyen: 1) “Does AWS EDP/commit apply?”
Recommended response: “Marketplace purchases may count toward your AWS commitment depending on your AWS agreement. We can provide the product/offer details; your AWS rep can confirm how it applies to your EDP.”
Asset: Procurement one-pager (section: ‘When Marketplace helps’)
Owner: Soojin to confirm final wording.

Soojin Lee: Yep. I’ll add a “do/don’t” snippet in the enablement doc. (reaction :thumbsup: from Jordan Blake)

Stephanie Nguyen: 2) “Private offer / net terms / annual commit?”
Recommended response: “Yes — for Dedicated/Private and larger Hosted commitments we can do private offers in AWS/GCP Marketplace. Standard terms/discount guardrails apply; we’ll loop in Partnerships + Finance for approvals.”
Asset: internal AWS private offer notes + Procurement one-pager

Isabelle Laurent: Please also capture what we need from the rep to initiate: AWS account ID, payer, marketplace seller ID (if applicable), desired start date, term, and SKU. A bunch of deals stall because we’re missing basic info.

Markus Klein: 3) “What happens after Subscribe?”
Good simplified answer (what I’ve been saying): “Subscribe in Marketplace → you’ll be redirected to Redwood Console to sign in/create an account → we verify entitlement → you either create a new org or link to an existing org → create first API key → run a quickstart request.”
We should put this exact flow in the FAQ and in the listing ‘How it works’ section.

Ben Carter: +1. I’ll add explicit callouts:
- If entitlement isn’t available yet: wait/retry + check org email domain
- If already have org: choose ‘Link existing org’
- If multiple subscriptions: we support linking multiple entitlements to one org (with admin controls)

Rachel Kim: Can we be specific on entitlement timing? I’ve seen ~2–10 min typical, but sometimes 30+ min edge cases.

Dev Patel: Suggest wording: “Typically a few minutes; in rare cases up to ~30 minutes. If it’s been >30 minutes, contact support with your subscription ID.” Keeps it honest.

Stephanie Nguyen: 4) “Security packet / SOC2 / encryption / audit logs. Is it public?”
Recommended response: “We have a marketplace-ready security overview covering SOC 2 posture, encryption in transit/at rest, KMS options (where applicable), audit logs, retention, and shared responsibility. Some evidence is gated; we can share under NDA via the trust portal/process.”
Asset: Security documentation pack index + questionnaire responses draft

Soojin Lee: Please avoid “SOC2 certified” language. Use “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA” (or whatever Security approved). Marcus/Olga have been strict about this.

Lukas Meyer: 5) “Data residency / EU region?”
Recommended response: “Hosted supports region selection for supported regions; Dedicated/Private can be deployed in customer-selected regions/VPC/on-prem. We’ll confirm availability for your required geography.”
Asset: Data handling & residency page

Jordan Blake: Keep it simple but don’t overcommit on “guarantees” unless it’s in the SKU/terms. For regulated workloads, steer to Dedicated/Private.

Isabelle Laurent: 6) “Invoicing / who bills us?”
Answer: “AWS/GCP invoices you via Marketplace; Redwood does not send a separate invoice for marketplace usage (except as needed for support/PS per contract).”
Asset: revrec/invoicing notes (internal) + customer FAQ section.

Markus Klein: 7) “Support SLAs?”
Answer: “Support depends on the SKU/support tier purchased. Marketplace subscription includes standard support; enterprise support/SLA is available via Dedicated/Private offers (often via private offer).”
We should make sure the listing doesn’t imply 24/7 for everyone.

Dev Patel: +1. Also clarify where to open tickets. “Use Redwood Support portal/email; include marketplace subscription ID and org ID.” If it’s a marketplace portal issue (can’t subscribe), that’s AWS/GCP.

Ben Carter: 8) “Trial/credits through Marketplace?”
What’s the official stance? I’ve seen inconsistent answers.

Jordan Blake: Default: no “free credits” via Marketplace unless explicitly configured in the offer. For PLG trials, push direct signup. For procurement-mandated marketplace customers, we can do a low-commit private offer if needed.

Soojin Lee: I’ll add to FAQ: “Trials vary by marketplace/offer; contact sales for options.”

Stephanie Nguyen: 9) “Metering / unexpected billing dimensions?”
I had one prospect spooked by ‘dimension names’ on the AWS side not matching what they expected.

Isabelle Laurent: We should document: “Marketplace bills based on defined dimensions that map to Redwood usage meters (tokens/requests/capacity). Your Redwood Console usage dashboard shows the same underlying usage categories.”
Also add a line: “If you see a mismatch, contact support; we can reconcile.”

Markus Klein: 10) “Can we consolidate multiple cloud accounts into one Redwood org?”
Answer: “Yes, org is the billing/admin boundary in Redwood. You can link multiple entitlements/subscriptions (subject to admin permissions). If you need strict separation, create multiple orgs.”

Rachel Kim: + add: “SSO requirement before purchase?” Some enterprises ask if SSO is required to provision.

Ben Carter: Response: “SSO is optional; you can start with email/password and later enforce SSO (SAML/OIDC) for the org.”

Stephanie Nguyen: Action items:
- Soojin: incorporate approved wording into FAQ + enablement doc (EDP/commits, SOC2 wording, trial language)
- Ben: draft onboarding flow FAQ entries (Subscribe → entitlement → org link → API key) + entitlement timing language
- Isabelle: give me the “info we need for private offer” checklist + invoicing snippet for the internal one-pager
- Dev: support escalation blurb + what IDs to request from customer

Jordan Blake: Also add one more objection I keep hearing: “We need to route this through procurement but engineering wants to start now.” Our answer should be: “You can start with Hosted direct trial, then move to marketplace once procurement is ready” (where that’s allowed). Make sure we don’t create a compliance issue for regulated accounts.

Soojin Lee: Noted. I’ll add a “bridge plan” section with guardrails (and route regulated/Private prospects to SE).

Stephanie Nguyen: Keep dropping more as you hear them. I’ll compile into a single ‘Objections → responses → links’ doc for the sales team.

(reaction :memo: from Markus Klein)
(reaction :thumbsup: from Lukas Meyer)

preview:
Chris Osei: AWS review feedback came back (round 1). Dropping the key items here + proposed owners. Please react :thumbsup: if you can take, or reply w/ an alt owner.

Chris Osei: Summary from AWS team:
1) Missing artifacts: need Security Overview doc (public URL), Data Flow Diagram (image/PDF), Support contact + SLA statement, and EULA/terms link.
2) Wording changes: remove/soften comparative claims (“best-in-class”, “#1 latency”) unless we cite a public benchmark. Also avoid “SOC2 certified” phrasing.
3) Offer structure: clarify what’s included in each SKU vs add-ons; current copy reads like Dedicated/Private are features inside Hosted.
4) Screenshots: they want at least 3 product screenshots (console, usage dashboard, audit logs) w/ captions.
5) Metering/billing: ensure the metering dimension names in the listing match what’s configured in the AWS metering integration (they flagged 2 dimension labels as inconsistent).

Stephanie Nguyen: Re #2, they highlighted the sentence “industry-leading latency across all regions” as problematic. We can say “optimized for low latency and high throughput” (no superlatives) and link to our perf page if we have one.

Soojin Lee: Yep. I’ll do a copy sweep today. If we don’t have a public benchmark URL we should delete the numeric latency claims too.

Jordan Blake: Agree. Don’t get cute w/ claims in marketplace. Keep it enterprise-safe.

Camila Reyes: On screenshots: I can get 3–4 captures from Console (model catalog + routing policy, usage/cost breakdown, audit log viewer). Need final IA/URLs for captions.

Alex Martinez: We can point AWS to the docs-site Security Overview page once PR-3422 lands. I’ll confirm the final URL + get redirects if needed (don’t want to change after submission).

Naomi Feldman: For security wording: please don’t say “SOC2 certified.” Preferred: “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA/on request” + “controls aligned to ISO 27001” (only if we’re not implying certification). Also, AWS asked specifically for data retention defaults + audit log retention.

Chris Osei: Owners draft:
- Copy edits (remove comparative claims, clarify SKU boundaries): @Soojin Lee
- Security Overview + shared responsibility blurb + retention/audit statements: @Naomi Feldman (review w/ Dr. Aisha Rahman)
- Data Flow Diagram export (PDF) for submission: @Camila Reyes (source from Security/Eng if needed)
- Support + SLA language block + support contact email: @Ben Carter (loop CS)
- EULA/terms link + refund/credits language for marketplace: @Sofia Mendes
- Metering dimension label check (AWS listing ↔ internal meters): @Cole Summers
- Final docs URLs / redirects readiness: @Alex Martinez

Cole Summers: On #5: I know the mismatch they’re seeing. Our internal meter is `hosted_tokens` but the draft listing dimension says “Hosted - Tokens (Input/Output)”. AWS wants exact names. I can propose updated dimension strings that match what we’ll ship in the metering config.

Soojin Lee: Please send me the exact dimension strings you want in listing so I can align copy + the pricing table.

Cole Summers: Will do in ~1 hr. Also note: they flagged “Dedicated GPU Hours” dimension; we’re actually metering “Reserved Capacity Units” not literal GPU hours.

Sofia Mendes: For terms: AWS wants a publicly accessible EULA link. We can use our standard MSA/EULA page, but we need a marketplace addendum (billing via AWS, cancellation, refund policy). I’ll draft a short addendum and route to Michael Grant for sign-off.

Ben Carter: Support/SLA: for marketplace public listing, we should keep it simple: “Standard support via support@redwoodinference.com” + “Enterprise SLAs available for Dedicated/Private” (don’t state response times publicly unless we’re ready to commit).

Jordan Blake: +1. Don’t publish response times unless Legal + CS agree.

Camila Reyes: For the audit logs screenshot, do we have a customer-safe view w/ no PII? I can use staging with fake org.

Naomi Feldman: Use staging w/ synthetic data only. Also, AWS asked for “encryption at rest/in transit” statement. We can say TLS in transit + encryption at rest in cloud storage + KMS support for Private/VPC. Keep it generic (no algorithm name unless we’re consistent everywhere).

Alex Martinez: Re “public URL”: AWS reviewer specifically asked that the Security Overview not be behind a login wall. We can publish a high-level page + keep evidence/report links gated.

Chris Osei: Yep, that matches our publication plan.

Chris Osei: Direct quotes from AWS feedback (so we don’t lose nuance):
- “Please provide a customer-facing architecture or data flow diagram showing where customer prompts, outputs, and logs reside.”
- “Avoid comparative marketing claims unless supported by a publicly accessible citation.”
- “Clarify support contact and escalation process for enterprise customers.”
- “Dimension names in listing must match metering integration.”

Soojin Lee: Copy plan:
- Replace “industry-leading / best-in-class” w/ “optimized for”
- Remove specific latency numbers unless we link a public benchmark
- Split sections: Hosted API vs Dedicated vs Private with “Purchase via marketplace” bullets per SKU
- Add a short “How it works” (subscribe → link org → API key → usage + billing)

Chris Osei: Great. Camila—can you add captions that reinforce Hosted/Dedicated/Private difference?

Camila Reyes: Yep. I’ll draft captions and tag Soojin for review.

Naomi Feldman: For the security packet: we should ensure retention language is consistent:
- Prompt/response retention default (what we store + for how long)
- Configurable retention controls (if any)
- Audit logs retention
- “Customer data not used for training by default” (if we’re stating it publicly, wording must match existing policy)

Jordan Blake: Please keep “not used for training” statement consistent across all channels. Prospects ask about this constantly.

Chris Osei: Timeline: AWS said if we resubmit assets by EOW, we can stay in the current review window. Can owners target updates by Thursday 3pm PT?

Soojin Lee: I can get the copy edits + updated FAQ by tomorrow.

Cole Summers: Metering dimension names by today.

Sofia Mendes: Terms addendum draft by Thursday morning.

Alex Martinez: Security overview page URL + redirects by Thursday.

Camila Reyes: Screenshots + diagram export by Thursday (assuming we get the final data flow diagram from Eng/Security).

Naomi Feldman: I’ll provide the security wording + review the diagram by Wednesday.

Chris Osei: Action items (tracking here):
- @Cole Summers send canonical AWS dimension names + confirm Dedicated dimension semantics
- @Soojin Lee update AWS listing copy + SKU clarity + remove unsupported claims
- @Naomi Feldman provide marketplace-safe security language (SOC2 phrasing, encryption, retention, audit logs)
- @Camila Reyes capture 3–4 screenshots + export data flow diagram PDF
- @Alex Martinez confirm public docs URLs + redirect plan (no post-submit breakage)
- @Sofia Mendes draft EULA/marketplace addendum + refund/cancellation language
- @Ben Carter finalize support contact block

Chris Osei: Please reply in-thread if any of these are blocked. I’ll update the partner review tracker once we have the revised bundle.

title: #sales: 1769150033
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: quick PSA since this came up twice today — SOC 2 report requests should *not* be handled via email attachments / “can you just send it to me” 🙏

Stephanie Nguyen: ✅ Canonical process (SOC 2 + evidence):
1) Confirm NDA is in place (or get one executed). If they don’t have one → loop Legal via the deal room thread, don’t improvise language.
2) Open an evidence request using the playbook + ticketing flow (so GRC can track SLA + what we actually shared)
3) Only send the customer the approved link/secure share path once GRC confirms NDA + packages the artifact.

Stephanie Nguyen: Confluence playbook here (bookmark it): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/Security/soc2-customer-evidence-request-playbook

Stephanie Nguyen: TL;DR: if customer says “need SOC2 by Friday” → do NOT DM a PDF around. Create the request + tag #security-and-compliance / @Olga Petrov / @Daniel Carter if truly urgent.

Avery Johnson: +1, had a prospect asking me to forward “whatever you sent Acme” 😬 glad there’s a clean process.

Hannah Schmitt: for NDA — do we have a default form we can send, or always customer paper?

Daniel Carter: typically customer paper, but we can provide ours if needed. Either way: route through Legal + deal channel. Please don’t accept NDA terms in email.

Olga Petrov: also reminder: SOC 2 report is shared *under NDA only* and via controlled distribution. We track which accounts received which version.

Markus Klein: on the request intake — should SEs file it or AEs?

Olga Petrov: either is fine, but please make sure the request includes:
- account name + domain
- due date
- what they’re asking for (SOC2 report vs bridge letter vs pen test summary etc)
- deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) + any specific control gaps they’re worried about

Jordan Blake: and pls cite the current kit version in the deal notes so we don’t have “random PDF from last year” floating around.

Rachel Kim: +1 from CS side — once it’s shared, please drop the evidence request link in the account channel so we can reference later (renewals always re-ask)

Security GRC Bot: Reminder: SOC 2 artifacts are restricted. If you need access to the evidence folder or can’t locate the request form, follow the playbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/Security/soc2-customer-evidence-request-playbook

Stephanie Nguyen: thanks all. if anyone sees SOC2 PDF being forwarded around, call it out (kindly) + point back here. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1769102468
preview:
Jordan Blake: FYI I ran a recurrence clustering pass for May

ug (30
-day windows). Prioritized groups by SLA hits and customer escalations. Short summary + suggested owners below. Full notebook: https://redwood-internal/notes/recurrence-synthesis-2026-05-aug

Tara Venkatar: nice, can you paste the clusters + basic counts so we can scope lift?

Jordan Blake: sure 

```
cluster_key | incidents | sla_violations | cust_escalations
KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH | 21 | 8 | 3
FALLBACK_ROLLOVER_OSC | 15 | 6 | 2
GRPC_CONN_RESET_OLD_IMAGE | 12 | 4 | 1
AUTOSCALER_SUDDEN_SCALE_DOWN | 9 | 5 | 1
EGRESS_THROTTLE_THIRD_PARTY | 6 | 3 | 2
OTHER | 10 | 2 | 0
```

Query: `-- clustering by signature + time-window + customer-impact score` (see notebook). I also computed a recurrence half-life metric per cluster to know which will decay vs persist. KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH has the longest tail.

Ethan Zhao: i'll take KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH. plan: (1) per-tenant eviction caps + soft isolation, (2) adaptive admission for hot prefixes, (3) eviction latency SLOs + dashboard. Risk: Dedicated p95 might shift; will add feature-flag and 1-click revert. ETA: 3 weeks (2026-09-08).

Rosa Alvarez: i'll own FALLBACK_ROLLOVER_OSC. hypothesis: simultaneous pool degradation + aggressive rollback policy causes flip-flop. Action: add rollout cooldowns, per-pool fallback hysteresis, and a console gate that pauses rollouts above a fallback threshold. Also adding an eval suite for semantic drift during canary. ETA: 2 weeks (2026-08-25).

Tara Venkatar: for GRPC_CONN_RESET_OLD_IMAGE i can coordinate reprovisioning and a keepalive bump. will tag eng-infra and schedule a node-image retirement plan. ETA: 10 days.

Jordan Blake: I'll create epic INC-4722 and seed tickets. incidents-bot, please create INC-4722 and tag recurrence-analysis + priority-high.

incidents-bot: Created epic INC-4722: "Recurrence synthesis: May-Aug followups" and added labels: recurrence-analysis, priority-high. https://jira.local/browse/INC-4722

Ethan Zhao: action items to add to INC-4722:
- Ethan Zhao 
V: per-tenant eviction caps + admission control PR + load tests + rollback docs 

 due 2026-09-08
- Rosa Alvarez 

 fallback cooldowns + canary gate + eval prompts 

 due 2026-08-25
- Tara Venkatar 

 grpc keepalive bump + node-image retirement plan 

 due 2026-08-30
- Platform SRE (assign: @ops-oncall) 

 autoscaler safety nets + alert tuning 

 due 2026-09-01
- Integrations team 

 investigate third-party egress throttles + backoff guidance 

 due 2026-08-22

Rosa Alvarez: risk flags: fallback gating must avoid blocking urgent security rollouts. will add a bypass path with manual approval and audit log.

Ethan Zhao: for load tests i'll run 2x observed peak QPS and compare p50/p95/p99 and cache-hit ratio to baseline. will upload results to the epic.

Tara Venkatar: propose measurement plan for the sweep: baseline metrics (SLA violations/week, incidents/week, median MTTR), run weekly pulse check and report a decay curve. if recurrence count doesn't drop 50% in 6 weeks escalate to ops review.

Jordan Blake: good measurable goals. I'll add a dashboard tile for each cluster and a weekly automated summary email to incident-owners.

incidents-bot: Reminder scheduled: "Recurrence sync: INC-4722" on 2026-08-19 16:00 UTC.

Rosa Alvarez: let's meet Wed 2026-08-19 09:00 PT (16:00 UTC) 30m to lock owners and check initial blockers. Ping in this thread if anything needs to be moved earlier. :eyes:
